files.shortDescription: How the brain processes everything — the 7-step cycle from sensory input to action, chunks as the sole substrate, and all 15 observation parameters explained
files.fullDescription:
The most comprehensive mechanism file in the framework — maps how the brain-body system operates in a continuous 7-step cycle: Domain → Body-Input (17 categories) → Unconscious Processing → Feeling (bridge) → PFC (observe + orchestrate) → Body-Output → Domain feedback.

Key mechanisms: Chunk-System as the sole substrate (every pattern, habit, memory, and skill runs on chunks). Body-Feedback = continuous Reward/Dissonance evaluation with 3 Satiation Types (Cyclic/Tonic/Generative). Feeling = PFC observation interface with 7 fidelity layers. PFC = observer + indirect orchestrator with ~4±1 working memory slots, finite shared budget, and Hold/Suppress operations.

Covers: 15 observation parameters (cross-species vs human-specific), cortisol as universal amplifier across 7 modes, Agent-Mechanism (how the brain models other people using the same Simulation-Engine), development trajectory (same architecture, different chunk density), multiple timescales (milliseconds to decades run simultaneously), and 21 key reframes where v7.8 differs from mainstream psychology.

This is the 'source of truth' for how the system operates — recommended reading before any deep-dive sub-files.

files.shortDescription: Physical brain map — 4 neural zones (PFC, Cortex, Subcortical, Peripheral), timing hierarchy (50ms→200ms+), and why willpower has hard physical limits
files.fullDescription:
A physical architecture map of the brain-body system organized into 4 zones by PFC reach: Zone A (PFC itself, ~200ms+, slowest), Zone B (trainable cortex, ~150ms), Zone C (subcortical with limited PFC reach, ~12ms amygdala), Zone D (peripheral nervous system, ~50ms spinal reflex).

Key insight: PFC reach is a physical gradient, not binary. Zone A→B: strong top-down control. Zone C: indirect influence only (via vmPFC→amygdala uncinate pathway). Zone D: essentially zero reach (unique exception: voluntary breathing). This explains why willpower has fundamental limits — it is constrained by physical neural connectivity.

Covers: timing hierarchy (body processes BEFORE PFC is aware — Libet 1983), 17 receptor categories (exteroception + proprioception + interoception), individual hardware variation (DRD4 gene, COMT SNPs, oxytocin receptor density), and the key principle: hardware sets the range, chunk compilation chooses the position within that range.

Companion to Core-Software.md. For neuroscience researchers verifying claims against fMRI, lesion studies, and tractography. Detailed breakdowns in Neural-Architecture.md.

files.shortDescription: How to use AI with this framework — setup guide, 6-step interaction protocol, and 12 positions where AI trained on mainstream science will give wrong answers
files.fullDescription:
A practical guide for using an AI assistant to explore the Human Predictive Drive framework — covers setup (5 minimum files, ~6,200 lines), reading order, and a 6-step interaction protocol: Detect user background → Read relevant files → Adapt strategy → Present → Iterate → Deepen.

Includes a 'Danger Zones' section listing 12 specific positions where the framework differs from mainstream psychology and neuroscience. AI trained on mainstream data will get these wrong without reading source files (examples: dopamine ≠ reward, mirror neurons ≠ empathy module, cortisol ≠ stress hormone, prediction error ≠ reward).

Introduces the Dual Check principle: body-check alone is not enough — body evaluates pattern coherence, not truth. AI can amplify a coherent-but-wrong pattern. Domain-reality check (external feedback) is the final arbiter.

Includes 3 worked example conversations (non-expert, domain expert, parent), a navigation table mapping 12 common topics to specific files, and language style guidelines for adapting to different knowledge levels.

files.shortDescription: Complete reading progression — 6 tiers, 55 core files (~85,700 lines), from foundation to collective to domain
files.fullDescription:
The definitive reading guide for the Human Predictive Drive framework — organizes ~97 files into a structured progression across 6 tiers.

Tier 0 Foundation (8 files) covers minimum for AI interaction. Tier 1 Mechanism (14 files) deep-dives individual components. Tier 2 Entity (8 files) covers how the brain models entities and builds relationships. Tier 3 Observable Patterns (13 files) covers observation parameters — named patterns emerged from system interaction. Tier 4 Collective (6 files) covers emergent group infrastructure. Tier 5 Domain (6 files) covers external reality.

Each tier includes enrichment files for optional deeper exploration. Includes a Bridges section mapping 5 cross-system connections (Body↔PFC, PFC↔Domain, PFC↔Entity, Body↔Entity, Body↔Collective) and a Reference section with clarification, hardware, melody lens, and PFC detail files.

Notes 3 observation parameters without dedicated files: Disgust, Mastery, Passion (described in Core-Software.md §8).

files.shortDescription: Detailed physical map of brain regions — which structure does what, connects to where, how far PFC can reach each zone, plus bilateral architecture (corpus callosum, lateralization, split-brain validation)
files.fullDescription:
A precise physical map of the neural systems underlying the framework — replaces the vague 'Unconscious' box with a specific inventory: which brain region, what function, what connections.

Organized into 4 zones: (A) PFC — dlPFC, vlPFC, OFC, vmPFC, mPFC, ACC; (B) Cortical Modality Areas — visual, auditory, language (Broca+Wernicke), somatosensory, motor, cerebellum, insula; (C) Subcortical — amygdala, hippocampus, thalamus+TRN, hypothalamus, basal ganglia, VTA, LC, Raphe, PAG, NTS; (D) Peripheral — ENS (~100-500M neurons), spinal cord, ANS.

For each region: anatomical location, primary function, key connectivity pathways, and citable research. Every claim is verifiable. Covers PFC reach limits per zone, chunk compilation across all zones, and framework implications.

§7 Bilateral Architecture: all zones have left+right copies, corpus callosum as integration layer (~200M axons), lateralization is function-specific not person-specific (Nielsen et al. 2013), split-brain evidence validates PFC=Lawyer and 'unity = emergent from harmony' principle. Framework is hemisphere-agnostic by design.

Designed for researchers who want to challenge or extend the framework at the biological level. Companion to Core-Hardware.md (overview) and Neural-Processing-Flow.md (signal pathways).

files.shortDescription: The physical signal path through the brain — how sensory input travels from receptors through thalamus, cortex, chunk binding, PFC, and back out as behavior
files.fullDescription:
Maps the physical journey of every signal through the body-brain system — from receptor firing to behavioral output, with timing at each step.

Hardware flow: Sensor → Thalamus (sensory gateway, TRN attention gate) → Cortex (modality-specific processing) → Binding (multi-modal chunk activation) → Unconscious processing (chunk evaluation, VTA salience, body-feedback) → Feeling (integration → PFC-observable) → PFC → Body-Output → feedback loop.

Covers: why this hardware architecture exists (Compilable Architecture trade-off — general-purpose adaptability requires slow development), Compiled vs Fresh processing at the physical level, PFC = Lawyer not Judge at the structural level (PFC literally arrives after body has already processed), 5-Channel Input Vector, and the circuit-breaker mechanism.

Key insight: every mechanism in the framework (chunk, feeling, logic, imagination, planning) runs on this physical substrate. Understanding the substrate clarifies why some things can be changed and others cannot.

files.shortDescription: 6 encoding channels of the brain — how chunks store information across Visual, Auditory, Somatic, Motor, Emotional, and Communication modalities simultaneously
files.fullDescription:
Modality = the encoding channel through which the chunk-system stores information. A single chunk isn't stored in one place — it's distributed across multiple modalities simultaneously. Chunk depth = how many modalities it occupies: verbal-only = shallow, multi-modal = deep.

Covers: 6 modalities (Visual, Auditory, Somatic, Motor, Emotional, Communication/Language — 5 experience + 1 communication modality), hardware basis (each = a physical brain region in Zone B), chunk × modality interaction (same concept stored differently per person), 3 types of modality interaction (reinforcement / compensation / conflict), and modality-based thinking direction (vertical = analytical vs horizontal = creative).

Also covers: modality development by age (which channels come online when), and therapy matching (which modality to engage for which kind of chunk change — e.g., trauma in somatic modality can't be reached by verbal therapy alone).

Key insight: language (Communication modality) is dissociable from PFC — 'knowing what to say' and 'actually knowing' can be two different things.

files.shortDescription: Why sound is the only sensory channel that affects all observation parameters — 6 unique hardware properties of the auditory system explained
files.fullDescription:
A deep dive into why the auditory system is architecturally unique — the only sensory modality with 6 hardware properties no other channel has: always-on 360° reception, temporal precision, direct limbic bypass, involuntary arousal coupling, cross-modal entrainment, and social signal priority.

Because of these 6 properties, sound influences all observation parameters (Threat, Novelty, Boredom, Connection, Status, Meaning...) in ways that visual, touch, or other modalities cannot replicate.

Covers: evolutionary logic (sound = temporal sentinel, vision = spatial tool), cross-parameter mapping (which sound properties activate which parameters), and the distinction between auditory as a hardware channel vs the observation parameters it influences.

NOTE: This file covers WHY auditory is architecturally unique. Music neuroscience specifics are in Drill-Sound-Brain/ (12 files). Auditory development timeline is in 04-Auditory-System-Arc.md.

files.shortDescription: What the framework does NOT know — 5 specific knowledge gaps between behavioral-level claims and the underlying biology, as a research roadmap
files.fullDescription:
An honest mapping of what the framework knows vs. doesn't know — organized as a 5-layer gap analysis using a computer analogy: framework operates at Layer 4-5 (behavior/function), but the driver layer (molecular biology) between receptors and chunks remains a blackbox.

The 5 gaps: (1) Body-input completeness — which signals actually reach processing; (2) Signal transduction 'driver layer' — the molecular path from receptor activation to chunk change; (3) Chunk substrate — the fundamental gap between behavioral patterns and neural implementation; (4) Integration→Feeling→Consciousness — how body signals become PFC-observable; (5) Hormone combinatorics — how multiple hormones interact simultaneously.

Also covers 2 complexity dimensions (scale: individual→collective, hardware variance: person-to-person), framework as a research roadmap (each gap = a testable direction), and how AI can act as a dynamic drill tool.

Key message: these gaps don't make the framework wrong — they mark where it transitions from evidence-backed claims to synthesis and hypothesis.

files.shortDescription: Foundation of the entire framework — what body-base is, 3 hardware foundations, compilable architecture, and how every sub-system connects
files.fullDescription:
The entry point for the entire Body-Base/ folder (~60+ files) — consolidates foundational principles without duplicating the detail in sub-files.

Covers: 3 Hardware Foundations (general-purpose reward, compilation mechanism, social hardware) → why they lead to Compilable Architecture (can adapt to any environment, but needs 15-20 years to compile). Body-Need 2-source aggregate: hardware baseline + chunk dynamics. Compiled vs Fresh as the primary processing axis. 3-Layer Evolution: Hardware → Compiled → Cultural.

Also covers: Unified Model 3+1 (Body-Base + PFC + Domain + Trust bridge), Body-Feedback overview (16 files, dual-pull, 5 preconditions, interface loop), 2-tier calibration (why body signals are trustworthy ~90% of the time), and the Circuit Breaker (how body self-detects calibration errors).

Key principle: body reacts first, PFC observes after. The order never reverses. Read this file before going into any sub-file.

files.shortDescription: Why body signals are trustworthy — the 2-tier calibration mechanism, 2 pathways, and 3 failure modes that explain when body can be wrong
files.fullDescription:
The meta-foundation file: answers the question every framework reader eventually asks — 'If the body can be wrong, why should I follow it?'

3 core questions answered: (1) Why does body 'know' a new song is good before PFC does? (coherence evaluation — body checks if the pattern fits existing chunk structure). (2) Why does following body reward tend to map domain reality correctly? (Darwinian calibration: coherence ≈ survival). (3) Why ~90% accurate? (2-tier + 2-pathway calibration model).

Covers: 2-tier calibration (Darwinian survival tuning + Hebbian domain-contact feedback), 2 pathways (active domain contact + trust-injected), Compiled vs Fresh axis, recursive property (existing chunks filter/amplify future learning), and 3 failure modes where body is wrong (evolution lag, false chunk baseline, schema override).

Key GPS analogy: body is the GPS device, domain reality is the satellite signal. GPS is trustworthy BECAUSE it was calibrated against satellite data. When they diverge, the satellite wins (Dual Check principle).

files.shortDescription: How the body assigns emotional value and how it spreads through connected concepts — valence definition, 4 formation sources, and chain propagation mechanism
files.fullDescription:
The central reference for valence in the framework — defines what valence IS, how it forms, and how it spreads through schema networks.

Valence = the body's continuous emotional assessment of anything: positive (approach), negative (avoid), or neutral. Key: valence is per-entity and changes with experience and context.

Covers: 4 valence sources (direct body-base reward, Self-Pattern-Modeling observation, anchor-schema inheritance, Trust-Inject from external source), 3 update types (Event / Decay / Forced), and valence bias (chunks filter what gets noticed).

The propagation mechanism: abstract things (math, money, ideology) get valence from chain propagation — math → grade → university → job → income → body-feed. Positive valence at the chain's end propagates BACKWARD. PFC Blindness: PFC cannot see the full chain — it only sees the activated endpoint.

Companion: Entity-Valence-Dynamics.md = how valence BEHAVES per entity over time.

files.shortDescription: How your body's emotional value for a specific person changes over time — structural vs current valence, habituation, phantom resonance, and why distance reveals hidden love
files.fullDescription:
A detailed analysis of how valence (the body's emotional assessment) behaves differently for each specific entity over time — companion to Valence-Propagation.md.

Key distinctions: Structural valence (stored aggregate built over years, invisible to PFC until disrupted) vs Current valence (per-moment activation, what PFC sees). Hardware-Subsidy (body subsidizes processing for close entities — parent→child gets MAX subsidy, professional gets NONE), meaning VTA habituation masks structural valence.

Covers: 3 Firing Modes (Maintenance / Chunk-Miss / Context-Trigger), mixed valence dynamics (Love + Hate can coexist), 'new appreciation' mechanism (why distance reveals hidden love — removes VTA habituation), nostalgia mechanism (3-component: decay asymmetry + contrast + articulation), extreme valence (Love↔Hate boundary conditions), Phantom resonance (4-factor: compilation × hardware-subsidy × duration × decay asymmetry), and technology's impact on valence formation.

v1.2 addition: §2.4 MECHANISM NOTE — 'entity's state = my state' is DESCRIPTION (what PFC observes), not mechanism. The actual mechanism = gap-fillability baseline violated. Proof: 'xa mẹ mới biết thương' — mom's state unchanged but child feels pain = baseline violation, not state-sharing.

Builds on Valence-Propagation.md v4.0 (what valence IS). This file = how valence BEHAVES per entity.

files.shortDescription: Cortisol is not a 'stress hormone' — it's a change-readiness amplifier with 7 modes, and why direction matters more than level
files.fullDescription:
A comprehensive reframe of cortisol's role: not 'stress hormone to reduce,' but a change-readiness amplifier — no cortisol = system offline, moderate = optimal performance, excess without repair = damage.

Key insight: cortisol is a SUSTAINER (arrives 20-30 minutes after NE + Adrenaline), not a CAUSE of stress or pain. Pain comes from 3 separate sources: nociception, mismatch, and recalibration. Cortisol only accompanies them.

Covers: 7 cortisol modes (IDLE → LAZY → ACTIVE → FOCUSED → PUSH → FREEZE → CRASH), direction > level insight (novelty cortisol → approach behavior; threat cortisol → avoidance behavior — same level, opposite outcomes), HPA paradox (chronic high cortisol = PFC damage), compile direction gate (cortisol at compile-time locks in the direction of new chunks), and pathology (trauma loop 4-stage model).

Also includes: 10 touchpoints where cortisol participates in the framework cycle, optimization guidelines, and open research questions.

files.shortDescription: How the body deeply bonds with another person — mechanism for when someone becomes structurally part of your body-base, covering 4 bond types and loss dynamics
files.fullDescription:
A mechanism deep-dive into how the body forms deep structural bonds with other people — when compiled attachment chunks become deep enough that the entity becomes part of body-base itself (extension, entanglement, or mixed coupling).

Covers: 2D coupling model (Depth × Direction), 4 bond types (parent→child, child→parent, romantic, friendship) each with different hardware-subsidy levels (MAX→MODERATE→TEMPORARY→NONE), 3 coupling phases, 3 coupling paths, and compile_rate with 8 accelerators.

Also covers: Resonance Decline mechanism (2 Forces + 1 Fuel + Gap Drift), Phantom coupling (why feelings persist after the person is gone), 'Emptiness' 7-TP model (what happens when a deeply coupled entity is lost), anti-compiled-suppress dynamics, and why gap cloning is architecturally impossible.

Companion files: Entity-Compiled.md (neural reality), Bond-Architecture.md (4 bond types × mechanism), Love-Unified.md (observation-level analysis of love types).

files.shortDescription: Why and how two people's body systems interact — 8 foundational principles covering detection, evaluation, interaction chain, and what makes bonds sustainable vs dissolve
files.fullDescription:
The 'missing mechanism layer' that all inter-body files implicitly assume — formalizes how two body-base systems detect, evaluate, interact, and either sustain or dissolve their connection.

8 foundational principles: (1) 3 hardware foundations → Compilable Architecture as WHY; (2) Body-Need = 2-source aggregate (hardware + chunk dynamics); (3) Compiled vs Fresh = the real processing axis; (4) 3 independent cost sources; (5) Full interaction chain (Detect → Evaluate → Extend → Outcome → Compile/Dissolve); (6) 5-Channel Input Vector (visual, auditory, tactile, olfactory, internal resonance); (7) PFC = Lawyer not Judge (PFC rationalizes body vote, does not override it); (8) Entity-Compiled reframe (3 subtypes).

Also covers 3-Layer Evolution (Hardware → Compiled → Cultural). This file is the source-of-truth that Self-Pattern-Modeling, Connection, Empathy, and Agent-Mechanism all cross-reference.

files.shortDescription: Trust — compiled prediction about entity's gap-fill reliability: the meta-dimension that modulates all valence, with asymmetric build-slow/collapse-fast dynamics
files.fullDescription:
Trust is the meta-dimension in the valence profile per-entity — a compiled prediction about whether an entity will fill gaps RELIABLY over time. Distinct from valence (liking): you can fear God (negative valence) but trust God completely (positive trust), or like a new friend (positive valence) but not yet trust them with secrets (trust not yet compiled).

Trust builds SLOWLY through months/years of consistent gap-fill experiences but collapses FAST through a single betrayal — an architectural asymmetry reflecting the different compilation mechanisms for formation (gradual Hebbian strengthening) versus destruction (single high-salience violation event).

3 sub-dimensions: Competence (CAN entity fill this gap type?), Benevolence (DOES entity intend to fill my gaps?), Integrity (WILL entity follow through consistently?). 4 formation sources. 5-source maintenance depth model (absorbed from Guilt Drill §6.1). Default → Calibrated 4-phase developmental arc.

Trust × Valence interaction: normally co-occur (trust + like) but 6 mechanisms explain the co-occurrence across 3 levels, plus break conditions where they diverge. Trust × Power/Status: status gates access, which gates trust formation opportunity. Trust in Coordination-Node: position-trust vs person-trust distinction.

Formalized from Drill-Trust-Valence-Interaction v1.1 + Guilt Drill §6.1 (both absorbed, originals in backup/). Cross-references: Valence-Propagation §2 (brief definition), Collective-Body §5 (collective application), Coordination-Node §2.2/§3.4 (node application), Entity-Access §1 F3 (compiled form = Access Confidence).

files.shortDescription: The complete chunk system reference — how mental units form, connect, and operate, covering the 4-phase lifecycle, 2 operators, and expert vs beginner differences
files.fullDescription:
The central reference for the entire chunk system. Defines chunk = 'strength-weighted associative network compiled through experience' — the brain's basic unit of knowledge, habit, and pattern.

Covers: Chunk formation (4 compile mechanisms: repetition/emotional peak/multi-modal/sleep), 4 connection types, activation dynamics, anchor-decay model, labels and logic-planning enablement, the 7-step Discovery Lifecycle, and 2 operators (unconscious ~95% + PFC ~5%).

Key insight: experts have 50,000+ compiled chunks in their domain (Chase & Simon 1973); a novice handles each component explicitly where an expert handles everything as 1 meta-chunk. PFC 'frees up' proportionally to compile depth.

Synthesizes 44+ files (~48,600+ lines of deep analysis). For detailed mechanisms, follow cross-references at each section. v2.3.

files.shortDescription: v3.0 Architecture reframe: ALL compile = Exposure → Hebbian (1 Engine). 3 Compile Types = dominant modulator configurations. Trust = amplifier, not gate
files.fullDescription:
v3.0 ARCHITECTURE REFRAME: Moves from taxonomy-first (v2.0) to mechanism-first view. ALL compile runs on 1 Engine: Exposure → Hebbian. 3 Compile Types (Experience/Expertise/Trust) = labels for which MODULATOR is dominant, not which engine runs.

Key reframe: Trust = AMPLIFIER on gradient Mức 0-5 (Entity-Access), NOT binary GATE. Reconciles Chunk.md §2.3 'gate' language with Entity-Access 'gradient' model.

New in v3.0: Multi-stream compile (Content/Value/Entity/Behavior run in PARALLEL on same engine). Feedback loop: bidirectional but asymmetric (PFC→Entity-Valence slow/costly, Entity-Valence→PFC fast/free). Evolutionary gradient: engine conserved across species, modulators accumulated.

Kept from v2.0: 3 Hardware Constraints (why brain compiles short), 6 Trade-offs, 4 Pathways (same output, different mechanisms), interactions. Sleep maintenance → Compile-Sleep.md (companion file). ~50 citations. ~1,634L.

files.shortDescription: Sleep = offline maintenance system in Compile Architecture. 6 mechanisms: ~1.5 exposure-based, ~4.5 optimization-based. Sleep ≠ just another exposure source
files.fullDescription:
Detailed analysis of sleep's role in Compile Architecture. Core thesis: Sleep is NOT simply 'exposure source #4' — it is an OFFLINE MAINTENANCE SYSTEM.

6 mechanisms classified by exposure vs optimization: SHY (Synaptic Homeostasis — optimization), Active Consolidation (hippocampus→cortex transfer — partial exposure), Emotional Decoupling (strip acute valence tags — optimization), Gist Extraction (abstract patterns → Background-Pattern — optimization), Creative Linking (remote associations — optimization), Replay (reactivation — partial exposure). Net: ~1.5 exposure-based, ~4.5 optimization.

Compile Engine (Hebbian) = BUILD house (waking). Sleep = MAINTAIN house (offline): clean debris, transfer temp→permanent storage, repaint, simplify blueprints, connect new rooms to old, test-run systems.

Sleep × Architecture interaction: Compile Engine, Entity-Valence Bias, Exposure Channels, PFC Modulation. Sleep deprivation = architecture degradation (PFC degrades FIRST — adenosine accumulation). Waking rest = complement (~30%), NOT replacement. ~40 citations. ~1,317L.

files.shortDescription: How accumulated chunk patterns become invisible biases — link density as the second dimension of compile depth, and why chronic patterns are so resistant to change
files.fullDescription:
Defines Background-Pattern = accumulated chunk-network pattern built through experience + sleep gist extraction over long periods. Link density becomes so high it soaks into every new chunk processed. PFC CANNOT observe it directly — too dispersed to surface.

Extends Schema.md's depth model with a 2nd dimension: Compile Depth × Link Density. This 2D model explains: why chronic childhood trauma is harder to treat than isolated trauma events; why cultural patterns persist even when each chunk is individually weak; why expert intuition is inexplicable; why therapy takes years.

v2.0 additions: Triple Bias (Self-Pattern-Modeling: Retrieval + Template + Interpretation biases all three simultaneously), Background-Pattern × Structural Valence, × Entity-Compiled (trait-level), × Entity-Access (Mức 0-5), × Bond-Architecture (per-bond integration), × Boredom (compiled environment).

Same mechanism explains trauma, expertise, culture, and personality simultaneously.

files.shortDescription: Agent Mechanism — the 4-function system (detect, model, simulate, calibrate) brain uses to predict and navigate key agents
files.fullDescription:
Agent Mechanism is the brain's dedicated prediction system for entities that matter — parents, partners, rivals, mentors. Distinguished from the entity itself: this file covers the FUNCTION (4 operations), not the agent person.

Covers: the 4 core functions (Detect = gap-relevant signal scan; Model = build predictive schema; Simulate = run Simulation-Engine on entity; Calibrate = update model on prediction error), 11 sub-files in the Agent-Mechanism folder, and how this system integrates with Valence-Dynamics, Entity-Access gradient, Self-Pattern-Modeling, and Bond-Architecture.

Central distinction: 'Agent' (the entity: bố, mẹ, bạn) vs 'Agent Mechanism' (the brain function). This file is the integration hub — understanding one section requires familiarity with the sub-files.

files.shortDescription: Self-Pattern-Modeling — APPLICATION-1 on Simulation-Engine: how brain builds and updates a predictive model of other agents using 3 dimensions
files.fullDescription:
Self-Pattern-Modeling is the brain's dedicated mechanism for predicting other agents — APPLICATION-1 on the Simulation-Engine (renamed from Match to Modeling to clarify it builds a structural model, not just pattern-matches).

Covers: 1 mechanism × 3 dimensions (Pattern-Recognition = detect recurring agent signatures; Gap-Prediction = predict what gaps this agent fills; Compiled = how the model shifts from Fresh to compiled-automatic), how Self-Pattern-Modeling integrates with the shared Simulation-Engine substrate (training Self-Pattern-Modeling improves all simulation applications), 42 drill insights, and 57 citations.

Key distinction from empathy: Self-Pattern-Modeling = predictive model building (cold, structural). Empathy = felt resonance response (warm, evaluative). Both can run simultaneously but are separate operations.

files.shortDescription: Entity-Access — gradient model of how brain builds predictive access relationships with specific entities, from stranger to core dependency
files.fullDescription:
Entity-Access describes a continuous 0-5 gradient measuring how deeply a specific entity has been compiled into the brain's gap-prediction system. Mức 0 = stranger (no compiled access model); Mức 5 = excess territory (access overrides calibration).

Covers: the 3-Factor Model driving access movement (Engine Mode × Gap-Body-Need × Access Confidence), how access gradient maps onto felt experience (comfort, trust, longing, dependency), why the same entity can sit at different gradient positions across time periods, and how Entity-Access gradient maps onto the mPFC ventral-dorsal gradient (self-close-distant).

Key distinction: Entity-Access ≠ Entity-Valence (positive/negative evaluation). A Mức 4 entity can have mixed or negative valence — access is about prediction depth, not emotional tone.

files.shortDescription: Entity-Compiled — how close agents get structurally encoded in body-base at neural level, beyond schema and beyond valence
files.fullDescription:
Entity-Compiled covers the deep mechanism by which the brain compiles a close agent (parent, partner, close friend) into body-base at structural level — meaning their gap patterns become part of the prediction substrate itself, not just a schema or a valence tag.

Synthesizes 8 research streams: mirror neuron system, simulation theory, shared neural representation, oxytocin bonding, attachment neuroscience, grief neuroscience (grief = structural loss not emotional loss), Hub-and-Spoke architecture (key person = cognitive hub), and social baseline theory.

Key insight: 'Mẹ tôi đau = TÔI đau' is a neural reality, not a metaphor — but v1.2 clarifies this is DESCRIPTION, not mechanism. The actual mechanism = gap-fillability baseline violated. Compiled entity shares body-base circuits because entity fills many gaps uniquely → becomes part of baseline. Grief A+B+C model consistent with unified loss formula: Loss pain = Chunk-Miss on compiled baseline × predicted unfillability. Same mechanism from object loss (chopsticks) to entity loss (child), different parameters.

This explains why grief is more disorienting than losing an object, why long-term partners show synchronized cortisol, and why death of a core entity causes genuine cognitive reorganization.

files.shortDescription: Entity-Access-Calibration — 3-layer system that keeps entity-access gradient accurate through ongoing prediction error feedback
files.fullDescription:
Entity-Access-Calibration describes how the brain self-regulates access investment in specific entities — preventing both under-investment (missing real support) and over-investment (excess dependency).

Covers: 3-Layer calibration architecture (Hardware-Subsidy layer = bias toward compiling; Exit Cost layer = signal weight on withdrawal; Fresh-Evaluation layer = conscious override), why Exit Cost functions as a signal amplifier rather than just friction, and Hardware-Subsidy as a systematic bias (some entity types compile more easily regardless of actual gap-fill quality).

Practical implication: explains why leaving relationships that provide minimal gap-fill is still painful (Exit Cost signal), and why Hardware-Subsidy creates attachment to entities that objectively underserve our needs.

files.shortDescription: Entity-Access-Excess — when entity-access reaches Mức 5, activating the same neural circuits as substance addiction
files.fullDescription:
Entity-Access-Excess covers the territory beyond healthy entity-access (Mức 5 on the 0-5 gradient) — where the entity-gap prediction system enters a state structurally identical to addiction: same VTA/NAcc circuits, same tolerance mechanisms, same withdrawal phenomenology.

Covers: 3 origins of excess (Hardware-Subsidy compiles too fast; Gap-Body-Need too large relative to other fill sources; Exit Cost accumulation trapping); why excess is not a character flaw but a structural outcome of normal mechanisms operating under specific conditions; and the key clinical insight that treating excess requires addressing the underlying gap rather than removing the entity.

Distinguishes excess from dependency: dependency = high access within calibrated range; excess = access overrides calibration entirely.

files.shortDescription: Bond architecture — how Entity-Compiled structures into 4 bond types and why gap cloning between bonds is structurally impossible
files.fullDescription:
Bond-Architecture maps one mechanism (Entity-Compiled) onto four distinct bond types: Attachment, Affiliation, Alliance, and Parasocial. Each type has different gap-fill profiles, compilation thresholds, and decay patterns.

Key structural finding: gap clone across bond types is IMPOSSIBLE — the same gap cannot be served by a different bond type without recompilation. This explains why a friendship cannot simply 'replace' a romantic relationship even when both people are present and willing.

Covers: M1-M4 mechanisms per bond type, By-Product-Gap-Resonance × bond type, Resonance Decline forces (2 Forces + 1 Fuel), and how bond-switching works at the structural level.

files.shortDescription: By-Product-Gap-Resonance — mutual gap-filling that emerges as a by-product when two entities pursue their own goals simultaneously
files.fullDescription:
By-Product-Gap-Resonance is an observation lens, not a drive or emotion. It describes the structural condition where two entities' independent goal-pursuit incidentally fills each other's active gaps — creating sustained resonance without either party intending it.

Covers: 5 axes of resonance measurement, why resonance is strongest when it is a genuine by-product (not engineered), how this differs from deliberate gap-filling strategies, and why the mechanism explains phenomena like 'effortless connection' and 'working together feels right.'

Distinction: By-Product-Gap-Resonance = what emerges BETWEEN entities (observation lens). Entity-Access = what one entity builds toward another (gradient model).

files.shortDescription: By-Product-Gap-Resonance at 3 scales — same 1 mechanism operating as Pair resonance, Hub resonance, and Institutional resonance
files.fullDescription:
By-Product-Scale shows how one mechanism (mutual by-product gap fill) produces qualitatively different phenomena at three social scales: Pair (2 entities), Hub (1 central node + network), and Institutional (distributed, anonymous).

Key insight at Hub scale: Prestige = genuine resonance at scale. A person becomes a hub node not by pursuing status but by incidentally filling gaps for many people simultaneously. This explains why manufactured status feels hollow while earned prestige feels authentic.

Covers: hardware shift across scales (myelination, oxytocin reach limits), By-Product-Gap-Resonance v1.4 integration, and technology's role in extending hub reach beyond physical constraints.

files.shortDescription: Resonance-Per-Entity — how by-product gap resonance manifests differently across 5+ entity profile types using the same 3-layer model
files.fullDescription:
Resonance-Per-Entity maps the universal resonance mechanism onto distinct entity profiles, showing why the same by-product gap-fill process produces different phenomenology for different relationship types (parent-child, peer, mentor, romantic partner, parasocial, AI).

Covers: 3-Tầng model (Supply layer = what gaps entity fills; Mechanism layer = how resonance is generated; Dynamics layer = how it changes over time), 14 parameters governing per-entity resonance, Hardware-Subsidy spectrum across entity types, Compilation Chain (how multiple lower-Mức entities can substitute for one Mức 4 entity), Phantom 4-factor (why resonance persists after entity is gone), and technology's role in extending entity reach.

Key application: explains why AI can produce genuine resonance response (Supply layer active) without Compilation Chain (no body-base compile) — a fundamentally different relationship topology.

files.shortDescription: Resonance-Sustainability — 4-layer model predicting which conditions sustain vs. decay by-product gap resonance over time
files.fullDescription:
Resonance-Sustainability identifies the structural conditions that determine whether resonance between two entities persists or declines — independent of goodwill or effort.

Covers: 4-Layer model (Hardware-Subsidy layer, Gap-Body-Need layer, By-Product layer, Compiled layer), 3 primitive conditions (Proximity × Duration × Agent-mode — all three required for initial compilation), 3 modalities of sustained resonance (Active, Ambient, Recalled), 4 types of productive silence (when absence strengthens rather than weakens resonance), and how technology disrupts the proximity constraint.

Central mechanism: resonance decays when by-product gap-fill stops — even if emotional attachment remains. This explains why co-workers who become close often drift after a job change, despite genuine care for each other.

files.shortDescription: Final synthesis of the entire chunk analysis project — unified lifecycle, all hypothesis verdicts, framework contributions, and falsifiable predictions (33+ files, ~43,000 lines)
files.fullDescription:
The culminating synthesis file for the Chunk-Analysis project. Integrates output from 33+ files (~43,000+ lines across 10 sessions) covering Child-Chunk-Development (F1), External-Development (F3), Internal-Processing (F4), plus already-drilled sub-folders.

Delivers: Unified Chunk Lifecycle (Compile → Install → Process → Plan), all hypothesis summary table with verdicts, framework contribution map, falsifiable prediction inventory, framework update recommendations (R-F1 + R-F3), remaining gaps, and Chunk.md v2.0 direction.

For detailed mechanism, follow cross-references to source files at each section.

files.shortDescription: Learning cycle — creating new chunks or connections emits 3 simultaneous signals (reward + fatigue + mild dissonance) resolved through sleep consolidation
files.fullDescription:
Formalizes H8: learning is not a discrete event but a cycle. When a new chunk forms or two chunks connect, 3 signals fire simultaneously — reward (prediction error satisfied), fatigue (metabolic cost of new synapse formation), and mild dissonance (new pattern conflicts with existing patterns until consolidated).

Covers: why learning simultaneously feels rewarding and tiring, sleep-mediated consolidation mechanism (multi-pass: hippocampus → cortex + synaptic pruning + REM offline processing), how the cycle iterates until baseline shift (the new chunk becomes compiled-automatic).

Originated from the question: 'why does creating new chunks cause fatigue despite feeling good?' The answer reveals the full 3-signal architecture of every learning event.

files.shortDescription: Agent gap-feed drill — 3-layer architecture (Layer 0 Entrainment PUSH + Layer 1 four hardware sources PULL + Layer 2 two mechanisms EVALUATE), per-entity profile, trajectory principle, Embodied-Mirror→Gap-Predict meta-process
files.fullDescription:
Deep drill analyzing HOW agents feed body-need gaps. Core finding: 3-LAYER ARCHITECTURE for agent-gap-feed.

Layer 0 — Body-State Entrainment (PUSH): entity present → body automatically synchronizes state. Unconscious, bidirectional, proximity-dependent. Entity-Compiled amplifies but is not required. Hardware-subsidy amplifies.

Layer 1 — 4 Hardware Sources (PULL): body HAS gap → entity fills. Sexual/aesthetic, Parental drive, Touch/Comfort, Safety. Each source = bounded circuit firing for 1 input type. Agent-irrelevant sources (Hunger, Temperature) become agent-relevant through COMPOUND with Touch/Comfort + Safety.

Layer 2 — 2 Processing Mechanisms (EVALUATE/PREDICT): ⑤ Novelty/Prediction-delta (immediate: VTA fires when actual ≠ predicted) + ⑥ Gap-Fillability Prediction (anticipatory: Self-Pattern-Modeling predicts entity's future impact on my gaps). Both are unbounded, cross-source mechanisms — fundamentally different from bounded sources.

Per-entity profile: each entity type has different primary source + mechanism weight. Friendship = mechanism-dominant (Novelty ⑤ = primary driver, all 4 hardware sources LOW). Mẹ→con = source-dominant (Parental ② primary + Novelty ⑤ auto).

Trajectory principle: ALL starting modes (Hardware/Limerence/Interaction/Schema) converge toward B-dominant destination via Novelty mechanism shifting from hardware-end to compilable-end.

Embodied-Mirror→Gap-Predict: unified 2-step meta-process. Step 1 Embodied-Mirror simulates entity's current state on own circuits. Step 2 Gap-Predict predicts entity's future impact on my gaps. 3 firing patterns (Embodied-Mirror alone / Gap-Predict alone / compound). 3 pain types (hardware gap / Gap-Predict anticipatory / Embodied-Mirror simulation).

9 observable signals. 8 extreme examples exposing independence and per-entity patterns. Gap body-need = INFINITE (gap = f(chunks), chunks = infinite). PROPAGATED into 5 framework files: Gap-Body-Need v2.0, Entity-Valence-Dynamics v1.3, Body-Coupling v3.1, Entity-Access v1.3, Resonance-Per-Entity v1.1.

files.shortDescription: Body-base boundary drill — compilation defines ownership (not connection), 9-position spectrum, chain mechanism, fingernail bridge, unified loss formula, entity state-sharing reframe
files.fullDescription:
Deep drill analyzing what defines the body-base boundary. Core finding: compilation, not neural connection, defines ownership. 9 positions from Entity-Owned (agents compiled into body-base) to PFC Direct (PFC's own tissue).

2D spectrum: compilation depth × neural connectivity. Viscera = decisive proof (physically closer than hand, but LESS PFC access). Rubber hand illusion: 120s Bayesian update = enough for temporary body-schema extension.

Chain Mechanism: PFC NEVER controls body directly — always through 5-7+ relay chains. Adding tool/prosthetic = adding 1 link to already-indirect chain. Quantitative extension, not qualitative change. Fingernail = natural bridge (body part with 0 neurons, compiled from birth).

Loss Mechanism: unified formula — Loss pain = Chunk-Miss on compiled baseline × predicted unfillability. Same mechanism from chopsticks (no pain) to child (devastating pain), different parameters (compilation depth × gap breadth × unfillability × duration). 'Entity's state = my state' = compressed prediction over gap-fillability, NOT literal state-sharing. Proof: 'xa mẹ mới biết thương' — mom's state unchanged, child feels pain = baseline violation.

3 converging proofs for 'compilation defines boundary': viscera (proximity ≠ access), chain (body already includes non-neural), rubber hand (120s = enough). 34 citations, 15 framework syntheses.

files.shortDescription: Bilateral architecture drill — Gazzaniga split-brain analysis, PFC ACCESS spectrum (7 cases from smooth to functional split-brain), framework audit (0 wrong, 5 strengthened)
files.fullDescription:
Session drill capturing deep analysis of Gazzaniga's split-brain research (1978, 2000, 2005) and its relationship to the Human Predictive Drive framework.

Covers: §1 Gazzaniga mechanism step-by-step (corpus callosum severed, Left PFC 'Interpreter' confabulates from available data). §2 Bilateral structure (what's cut vs not — cortical zones split, subcortical midline structures intact). §3 Lateralization is function-specific not person-specific (Nielsen et al. 2013, n=1,011 debunks left/right-brain people). §4 Unity = emergent from harmony, not structural — brain is multiple independent parallel systems. §5 PFC ACCESS spectrum — 7-case table from smooth operation through habit/schema conflict/signal too strong/hijack/multi-channel/background-pattern to split-brain, with 'functional split-brain' concept (compiled so deep PFC loses access despite intact corpus callosum). §6 Framework audit results: 9 claims checked, 0 wrong, 5 strengthened, 2 gaps fixed.

Key insight: split-brain patients and normal internal conflicts share the same mechanism — PFC access level is the single differentiating variable.

files.shortDescription: Drill — F1 Child-Chunk-Development commitment file — 7 constraint final verdicts, PFC-Inference Ladder formal contribution, and 10 framework update recommendations
files.fullDescription:
The commitment file for the Child-Chunk-Development drill series. Delivers formal verdicts for all 7 key constraints: Compile-Gradient, Compile-Rate-Formula, Emergent-Binding, PFC-From-Prenatal, Receptive-Productive-Gap, Label-As-Handle, Direction-At-Compile.

Key contributions: (1) PFC-Inference Ladder — a 5-level ladder for inferring developmental chunk states from observable events, with 10-marker empirical catalog and falsifiable predictions. (2) Hypothesis verdicts for Chunk-Substrate and Receptive-Productive-Asymmetry. (3) 10 framework update recommendations (R-F1-1 through R-F1-10).

Output contracts for downstream folders (F3, F4, 99-Master-Synthesis).

files.shortDescription: Drill — Master matrix of all F1 developmental events by body-input arc, with cross-cutting analyses by compile mechanism, ladder level, and hypothesis coverage
files.fullDescription:
An aggregation drill file — organizes all events analyzed in arcs 03-08 of the Child-Chunk-Development drill into master matrix tables. No new analysis; structured reference for the synthesis file.

Contents: 7 body-input arc tables (visual/auditory/motor/interoceptive/social/verbal), cross-cutting analysis by ladder level, hypothesis coverage table (verdict readiness check), compile mechanism distribution, open questions, and R-F1-* recommendations list.

Designed as a lookup reference: find a specific developmental event → §2 tables; see all L2 events → §3.3 distribution; check evidence for a specific constraint → §4 coverage.

files.shortDescription: Drill — Orientation sketch for the F1 Child-Chunk-Development series — 3-layer architecture map and 4-folder mechanism split (read this first)
files.fullDescription:
The orientation file for the Child-Chunk-Development drill — read before the 01-08 arc files. No deep analysis; provides the mental model needed to navigate the series.

Covers: 3-layer architecture (Body-Feedback L1 / Chunk System L2 / Feeling L3), 4-folder mechanism split (Child F1 / External F3 / Internal F4 / Master 99), 3 already-drilled sub-folders and their contributions (Learning-Cycle, Agent, Body-Feedback-Precondition), and F1 v3 mission with PFC hardware reframe + PFC-Inference Ladder introduction.

files.shortDescription: Drill — foundation reference proving PFC hardware is online from prenatal; 5 evidence pillars establish why behavioral failure does not equal structural PFC absence
files.fullDescription:
Load-bearing foundation for the F1 Child-Chunk-Development drill series. Corrects the Piaget-era 'PFC offline in infants' framing using 5 independent empirical pillars: synaptogenesis, fNIRS mPFC activation, resting-state network architecture, myelination timeline, and frontal consciousness signature.

Establishes the event-inference methodology used throughout arc files 03-08: list observable events, infer what chunks must have compiled for each event to be possible, classify with PFC-Inference Ladder (L0-L4). All subsequent arc files cite this file for the corrected framing.

files.shortDescription: Drill — womb environment characterization and prenatal chunk formation, establishing the baseline state at birth for the 6 body-input arc files
files.fullDescription:
Establishes the prenatal baseline state: womb ≈ homeostatic smooth (minimal domain-real dissonance), but prenatal chunk compilation begins before birth via auditory learning. Four independent lines of evidence (DeCasper & Spence 1986, Mehler 1988, Moon 1993, Kisilevsky 2003) confirm specific auditory chunks are present at birth — mother's voice, native language rhythm, heartbeat patterns.

Covers: birth moment physiology (placental→pulmonary circulation switch, first cry as brainstem survival reflex not emotional response), first sensory cascade post-birth (novel inputs hitting simultaneously), and why maternal soothing (familiar voice) provides partial smooth restoration via prenatal chunk resonance.

files.shortDescription: Drill — visual chunk compilation from birth to 24 months traced through Connect → Read → Control phases with PFC-Inference Ladder classification
files.fullDescription:
Traces visual system chunk compile mechanism from CONSPEC innate face template (birth) through mirror self-recognition (18-24 months). Analyzes 8 milestones via PFC-Inference Ladder (L0-L4): face preference, direct gaze, social smile, mother recognition, gaze following, object permanence, joint attention, mirror self-recognition.

Addresses Nút Thắt 1 (proto-chunk gradient: object file emergence as test case) and Nút Thắt 3 (multi-modal binding visual component). First of six body-input arc files; reads after 01 and 02 Foundation files.

files.shortDescription: Drill — auditory chunk compilation starting from fetal 26 weeks; phoneme discrimination trajectory provides the cleanest proto-chunk gradient evidence in the F1 series
files.fullDescription:
The only F1 arc where chunk compilation begins prenatally — 4 independent lines of evidence confirm specific auditory chunks at birth. Phoneme discrimination 0-12 months (Werker & Tees 1984): universal discrimination at birth → native-language-biased narrowing = cleanest 'proto-chunk → stable chunk' gradient evidence in developmental psychology.

Primary verdict file for Nút Thắt 1 (proto-chunk gradient vs discrete view). Also addresses Nút Thắt 3 (auditory component of multi-modal binding) and Nút Thắt 5 preview (receptive side of H11 Receptive-Productive Asymmetry).

files.shortDescription: Drill — motor chunk compilation (hand fine motor + locomotion + gesture) from birth reflexes to walking and proto-declarative pointing at ~24 months
files.fullDescription:
Traces two interleaved motor arcs: hand fine motor (palmar reflex → proto-declarative pointing → tool use) and locomotion (head control → walking). Motor system is output-dominant — distinctive from the receptive-dominant visual and auditory arcs.

Key contribution: formalizes the gesture-verbal gap (~4-8 months) within the same productive motor system — a clean within-body-input test of H11 Receptive-Productive Asymmetry. Provides bridge between motor evidence and the verbal production arc (file 08).

files.shortDescription: Drill — bladder interoceptive arc as the cleanest full L0→L4 PFC-Inference Ladder traversal; formalizes the 'frozen face' observable marker at L2
files.fullDescription:
The anchor case for the entire F1 drill: the same observable event (response to bladder fullness) spans the full L0→L4 PFC-Inference Ladder from birth to ~22 months. No other body-input arc spans all 5 levels as cleanly.

Primary home for 4 framework commitments simultaneously: Nút Thắt 2 (Precondition-2 Chunk-Substrate mechanism), Nút Thắt 5 (H11 Receptive-Productive Asymmetry), Nút Thắt 7 (Cortisol tagging critical period), Nút Thắt 4 (PFC hardware × chunks missing reconciliation). The key observable L2 marker — a 1-3 second facial freeze during play — provides the clearest empirical grip on 'PFC hardware present + action chunks absent' in a single event.

files.shortDescription: Drill — hunger, pain, thermal, and emotional interoceptive arcs applying the bladder methodology for cross-state comparison of compile rates and cortisol tagging patterns
files.fullDescription:
Applies the 06a analytical machinery to 4 other interoceptive states: hunger, pain, thermal, and emotional. Shorter than 06a because the methodology is already established — 06b focuses on state-specific events, compile rate differences, and cortisol tagging dynamics.

Key finding: bladder is not uniquely slow — all interoceptive states lag visual/auditory for the same structural reasons. Cross-state comparison strengthens Nút Thắt 2 (compile mechanism varies non-uniformly) and Nút Thắt 7 (each state has its own cortisol-tagging critical period window).

files.shortDescription: Drill — social recognition arc from CONSPEC face template at birth to self-as-agent at 24 months; delivers primary verdict on multi-modal binding mechanism
files.fullDescription:
Social is the only inherently multi-modal F1 arc — a single social event involves visual + auditory + proprioceptive + interoceptive + emotional channels simultaneously. Primary verdict file for Nút Thắt 3 (multi-modal binding mechanism) via E12 social smile keystone (6-8 weeks) and 4 converging mechanism lines.

Delivers 3 additional PFC-Inference Ladder L2 markers: stranger anxiety (E16), still-face Phase 3 puzzled reaction (E20), peek-a-boo anticipation (E30). Cross-references By-Product-Gap-Resonance.md for Resonance architecture applied to developmental events.

files.shortDescription: Drill — verbal production arc from cooing at 6 weeks to two-word combinations at 24 months; H11 full formalization and verbal-as-handle verdict
files.fullDescription:
Traces verbal production from cooing (6-8 weeks) through vocabulary spurt and two-word combinations (18-24 months). Primary home for two F1 framework commitments.

H11 (Nút Thắt 5): 7 converging evidence lines all showing productive bundle ≈ 3× receptive bundle compile time (ratio 1:3 across all body-input arcs). Verbal-as-handle verdict (Nút Thắt 6): hybrid mechanism — retrieval path + symbolic compression. Verbal labels are NOT a 5th modality and NOT constitutive of chunks; they are an enhancement layer added on top of existing body substrate.

files.shortDescription: Drill — 5 mechanisms by which the external world installs chunks into an individual (imitation, language, co-attention, cultural transmission, symbolic transfer)
files.fullDescription:
Core F3 file: formalizes the physical mechanisms by which external chunks get installed into an individual brain — the mechanism layer underlying all education, cultural transmission, and social learning.

Covers: 5 external install mechanisms with empirical grounding, how external install differs from internal discovery (external can install wrong chunks), when install succeeds vs fails (gap-direction dependency — chunk installs only if receiver has a gap it fills), and output contracts for downstream files. Tests hypotheses H3, H4, H7, H12.

files.shortDescription: Drill — F3 External Development commitment file — verdicts for Grammar-as-Anchor, Abstract-Metaphysical-Grounding, and language evolution driven by Gap 2 pressure
files.fullDescription:
The commitment file for the F3 Chunk-External-Development drill. Formalizes hypothesis verdicts for how external sources install chunks top-down.

Key verdicts: (1) Grammar-Versatile-Anchor (H3): grammar IS the most optimized external anchor system, enabling long-chain compile without individual effort. (2) Abstract-Metaphysical-Grounding (H4): abstract concepts ground in concrete chunk networks — not free-floating. (3) Gap2-Language-Evolution (H12): language evolved under Gap 2 pressure — coordinating long-future scenarios required shared verbal anchors.

Output contracts for F4 and 99-Master-Synthesis integration.

files.shortDescription: Drill — strategy layer for optimizing chunk transfer when sender and receiver have different chunk landscapes; analogy mechanism and 4 risks
files.fullDescription:
Extends 00-External-Mechanism.md with the optimization question: when sender's chunk landscape differs from receiver's, how to maximize successful install? Formalizes why 'saying' ≠ 'understanding.'

Covers: analogy mechanism (bridging via shared chunks) + 4 risks (false analogy, analogy-addiction, surface-deep confusion, compression loss), experience-based language as alternative, mini-gap chain theory (build bridges step by step), audience chunk detection before attempting install, and 2-force tension resolution.

files.shortDescription: Natural language architecture — structural analysis of language as a communication format comparing Vietnamese, English, and Mandarin across vocabulary, grammar, and syntax
files.fullDescription:
Dissects natural language as a communication format: vocabulary taxonomy (11 word classes), syntactic rules across 3 typologically different languages (Tiếng Việt / English / 中文 Mandarin), sentence → paragraph → discourse structure, and cross-language comparison.

Purpose: establish structural understanding of the most universal human chunk-encoding system as foundation before analyzing specialized formats (math, music, visual, code). All Mandarin examples include pinyin and Vietnamese translation.

files.shortDescription: Mathematical language architecture — mathematics as a communication format with its own vocabulary (symbols), grammar (operation order), and structural hierarchy
files.fullDescription:
Analyzes mathematics as a communication format — not to solve problems, but to understand its architecture as a chunk-encoding system. Mathematical language has vocabulary (numbers, operators, variables), syntax (expression construction rules), and hierarchical structure (expression → equation → proof → theory).

Key contrast with natural language: math encodes precise quantity/relationship; natural language encodes meaning and context. Both are external chunk-encoding systems operating on different axes of human knowledge representation.

files.shortDescription: Musical notation architecture — music as a communication format that uniquely encodes temporal-affective patterns that natural language and math cannot represent
files.fullDescription:
Analyzes music notation as a communication format architecture — not to teach music reading, but to identify what it uniquely encodes: temporal-affective patterns (emotion patterns unfolding across time). Staff notation, rhythm notation, and dynamic markings form specialized vocabulary for a dimension that text and math handle poorly.

Position in the 5-file series: Natural Language = sequential meaning, Math = precise quantity, Music = temporal emotion. Comparison reveals the complementary strengths of each format as a chunk-encoding system.

files.shortDescription: Visual and diagram architecture — visual formats encode spatial relationships and simultaneous overview that sequential text cannot convey; structural basis for why one image equals 1,000 words
files.fullDescription:
Analyzes visual and diagram communication formats (maps, flowcharts, bar charts, technical drawings, icons, emoji) as an architecture. Key unique property: spatial relationships + simultaneous overview — seeing all relationships at once, which sequential text cannot replicate structurally.

Covers: types of visual formats by what they encode, comparison with natural language and math, and the mechanism behind spatial comprehension. Completes the 5-format survey of human chunk-encoding communication systems.

files.shortDescription: Programming language architecture — the only executable communication format; structural comparison with natural language, math, music, and visual formats
files.fullDescription:
Analyzes programming languages as communication format architecture — for someone who already codes, this is structural analysis, not syntax teaching. Key unique property: executable communication — the only format a machine can directly act upon without human interpretation.

Covers: vocabulary taxonomy (variables, operators, control structures, functions, types), syntactic rules, program → function → module → system hierarchy, and comparison with all 4 prior formats in the series. Closes the Language-Structure-Analysis reference series with the most machine-oriented encoding format.

files.shortDescription: Drill — F4 Internal Processing sketch — mapping how existing chunks transform internally (insight, decay, feeling-intuition), establishing the F3/F4 boundary
files.fullDescription:
The F4 Chunk-Internal-Processing sketch file. Maps the territory before the detailed drill sessions. Establishes the boundary between F3 (external install of new chunks) and F4 (internal transformation of existing chunks).

F4 territory: logical connection (how chunks link to build inference), feeling-intuition gradient (how accumulated chunks produce intuitive knowing), chain-anchor-decay (how chunk chains weaken without reinforcement), insight mechanism (sudden new connections), and tacit knowledge.

Not for detailed mechanism — see arc files (01-06). Orientation + boundary-setting file for the F4 series. Tests hypotheses H2, H5, H6, H12.

files.shortDescription: Drill — how PFC intentionally connects existing chunks; tests H2 whether static logical linking is a distinct 4th connection type beyond resonance, causal, and contrastive
files.fullDescription:
Formalizes 4 types of chunk connections: Resonance (body-driven similarity), Causal (A leads to B), Contrastive (A vs B), and Static Logical (H2: deliberate PFC linking via rule). Tests H2: is PFC-intentional linking a structurally distinct 4th type?

Covers body-vote mechanism for connection validation (body confirms whether a PFC-proposed link feels 'right'), establishing the logical-linking operation that underlies all planning and argumentation. This connection type requires PFC to be deliberately active — not automatic.

files.shortDescription: Drill — 3 core chunk mechanisms: activation probability distribution, competitive re-linking, and trigger surface — foundational for all downstream chunk operations
files.fullDescription:
Formalizes 3 foundational chunk mechanisms used by all subsequent drill files and downstream framework sections: ① Activation probability (chunks fire as a strength-weighted distribution, not binary on/off); ② Competitive re-linking (chunks change connections through weight shift, not deletion — old connections weaken, new ones strengthen); ③ Trigger surface (why some chunks fire more easily — threshold, context match, recency, emotional tagging).

Cross-cutting mechanism file — applies equally to F1 (child development), F3 (external install), and internal processing operations. Emerged from a session insight that the probability mechanism had never been explicitly formalized.

files.shortDescription: Drill — macro lifecycle of the chunk system: from vague accumulation through labeling to logic-planning enablement and back to the next cycle
files.fullDescription:
Formalizes the macro cycle the chunk system follows: Accumulate (body experiences compile) → Vague emergence (multi-weak-signal convergence below articulation threshold) → Curiosity → Clarify → Label → Logic-Planning participation → Repeat.

Covers: Convergence Zone concept (what 'vague' IS at mechanism level — multiple weak chunk activations without a clear winner), why label is the prerequisite for logic-planning participation, Domain-Checked vs Self-Referencing selection pressure (accuracy convergence vs coherence convergence), and post-discovery cleanup (parsimony gain when competing near-identical chunks collapse). Companion to 01b (micro firing mechanism) — 01c is the macro system view.

files.shortDescription: Drill — the spectrum from clear body signal to vague intuition; tests H5 that vague knowing equals multi-weak-signal convergence
files.fullDescription:
Formalizes the gradient between clear body-knowing (certainty: 'I know this for certain') and vague intuition ('something seems off but I can't say what'). Tests H5: vague = multiple chunks each providing weak activation, none dominant enough for clear signal.

Covers: felt sense at the vague end (Gendlin Focusing), expert intuition at the clear end (Klein 1998 naturalistic decision making), and how feeling literacy training moves experiences from vague toward articulable. Connects to Somatic-Articulation-Loop as the mechanism for converting vague convergence to explicit knowledge.

files.shortDescription: Drill — why chunks need anchors, how chains form, and decay dynamics without anchor; tests H6 on Ebbinghaus decay and anchor type ranking
files.fullDescription:
Formalizes anchor mechanism: without a retrieval path (anchor), chunks follow Ebbinghaus decay regardless of how deeply they compiled. Anchors = retrieval handles that maintain access without requiring full recompilation.

Covers: 4 anchor types ranked by stability (body-first > semantic > verbal > location-context), chain formation mechanism (anchored chunk enables access to linked chunk, building retrievable chains), what happens when chains lose anchors (access failure, not deletion), and Ebbinghaus decay curve as the baseline for unanchored chunks. Tests H6.

files.shortDescription: Drill — mechanism behind 'something feels wrong' before being able to articulate what; ACC-based vague rightness/wrongness detection extending H5 into the evaluative domain
files.fullDescription:
Formalizes the evaluative vagueness mechanism: 'có gì đó không đúng' (something feels wrong) before being able to articulate what is wrong. Extends H5 (multi-weak-signal convergence) into the evaluative domain — multiple chunks each weakly signaling conflict or alignment, converging below articulation threshold.

Covers: anterior cingulate cortex (ACC) as conflict detection hardware, activation probability distribution in evaluative context, and how domain expertise raises signal-to-noise (experts get cleaner 'wrong' signals faster because their chunk landscape has higher resolution). Bridges Feeling Deep Analysis Theme D (clear evaluative cases) to the internal chunk mechanism.

files.shortDescription: Drill — How insight moments and tacit knowledge work at the chunk level, plus why 'sleeping on it' works and how Piaget stages are chunk accumulation thresholds
files.fullDescription:
Merges 3 related threads: (1) Insight/Aha moment — Type 2 connection (2+ old chunks suddenly link in a new way → novel chunk compiled), primarily unconscious (DMN incubation) — explains why 'sleeping on it' works. (2) Tacit knowledge — Polanyi's 'we know more than we can tell': extreme position on the feeling-intuition gradient, chunk network too dense to verbalize. (3) Cognition upgrade — chunk accumulation crosses thresholds to produce new capabilities.

H12 full treatment: language evolution driven by Gap 2 pressure — evolutionary pressure to coordinate on long-future scenarios created shared verbal anchor systems.

Piaget reconciliation: developmental 'stages' are not discontinuous jumps but threshold artifacts of continuous chunk accumulation.

files.shortDescription: Drill — F4 Internal Processing commitment file — final verdicts for 4 hypotheses: Static-Logical-Linking, Multi-Weak-Signal-Convergence, Anchor-Decay, and language evolution
files.fullDescription:
The commitment file for the F4 Chunk-Internal-Processing drill. Aggregates verdicts from 7 drill files (00-05 + 01b).

Hypothesis verdicts: (1) Static-Logical-Linking (H2): confirmed — 4 connection types form permanent associations enabling the inference ladder. (2) Multi-Weak-Signal-Convergence (H5): confirmed — multiple weak signals converge at threshold → intuition. (3) Anchor-Decay (H6): confirmed — chunks decay without reinforcement, verbal anchors slow decay. (4) Gap2-Language-Evolution (H12): confirmed — language evolved under coordination pressure.

Framework contributions: Compile-Gradient, Emergent-Binding, Label-As-Handle, Direction-At-Compile. Output contracts for 99-Master-Synthesis and bridge to Feeling Deep Dive.

files.shortDescription: Entry point for the 17-file Body-Feedback folder — 5 core claims, 6-layer pipeline visualization, gap system, signal architecture, and reading guide
files.fullDescription:
The synthesis entry point for the entire Body-Feedback/ folder (17 files, ~27,500 lines). Establishes 5 core claims before pointing to individual files: (1) body signal = function of 5 preconditions on interface loop, (2) body-feedback arises from 2 input sources × 3 chunk dynamics → Body-Need aggregate, (3) reward has 2 types (Evaluative/Direct-State) × 5 profiles × interaction model, (4) gap has direction + 3 satiation profiles + per-person distribution, (5) behavior = f(Gap-Distribution DEMAND × Action-Space SUPPLY).

Covers: full 6-layer pipeline visualization (sensor → thalamus → cortex → chunk binding → unconscious processing → PFC + inter-body layer), Dual-Pull architecture (Hardware pull vs Domain pull), 3 Genuine Discomfort Sources, Gap System overview (direction + satiation + distribution + behavioral prediction), Signal Architecture summary (reward types + dissonance types + calibration), Quality + Calibration section.

Includes a tiered reading guide for navigating all 17 files: Tier 1 (must-read foundation, 5 files), Tier 2 (mechanism deep-dives), Tier 3 (drill files).

Read this before any sub-file in the Body-Feedback/ folder.

files.shortDescription: How body signals physically arise — 2 input sources (sensory vs pattern-driven), 3 chunk dynamics (Shift/Miss/Gap), compound mechanism, and the Body-Need aggregate concept
files.fullDescription:
Classifies body-feedback signals by their CHUNK MECHANISM — the 4th axis alongside intensity, source, and precondition. Key concept: Body-Need = the aggregate output of chunk dynamics, the current state the body is demanding to be addressed.

2 input sources: Sensory-Driven (actual perceptual input from environment) vs Pattern-Driven (internal pattern activation without external trigger — imagination, memory, anticipation can all trigger body-feedback).

3 chunk dynamics: Chunk-Shift (existing chunk changes state — losing 100k feels like 'tiếc'), Chunk-Miss (expected chunk is absent — 'bạn thân bình thường hiện diện' is now gone → adds layer), Chunk-Gap (structure predicts something should exist but hasn't compiled yet — adds 'cay đắng' layer). Same monetary loss → different 'flavor' of dissonance depending on which dynamics fire.

Compound mechanism: multiple chunk dynamics fire simultaneously, each layer adding a distinct quality to the signal. Quality Baseline Shift: when body has compiled higher quality, prior baselines become insufficient — this explains many 'irrational' dissatisfactions.

Research: Crespi (1942), Schultz (1997), Berridge (2003), Amsel, Flaherty (1996).

files.shortDescription: The 5 conditions that must all fire simultaneously for any body signal to occur — why the same music rewards one person and leaves another cold
files.fullDescription:
Body-feedback signal does NOT fire automatically when a stimulus arrives. All 5 preconditions must be satisfied simultaneously — remove any one and the signal fails even if 'objective value' is present.

5 Preconditions: (1) Schema-pending status — is there an active gap or pending schema? (2) Chunk-Substrate adequacy — enough compiled chunks to decode the pattern? (3) Direction match — does the stimulus match the gap's specific directional shape? (4) Cortisol context tag — was the relevant chunk compiled under approach or avoidance context? (5) Compilation quality ceiling — which compile type (Experience/Expertise/Trust-Install) limits the achievable signal depth?

Same song → 5 different people → 5 different reactions: 'too strange' (Precondition-2 fail), 'nice!' (all 5 met), 'boring' (Precondition-3 fail — no delta), 'hate despite knowing it's good' (Precondition-4 — avoidance tag), 'feel nothing' (Precondition-1 — no active gap).

Scope qualifier: ALL 5 required for Evaluative reward/dissonance. Direct-State = simplified subset (hardware-level). Covers genesis vs decode split, dynamic zone model, and boredom disambiguation.

Companion: Body-Feedback-Mechanism.md (HOW), Body-Feedback-Label.md (VOCAB), Reward/Dissonance-Signal-Architecture (WHAT KINDS).

files.shortDescription: What kinds of reward exist and how they interact — Evaluative vs Direct-State reward, 5 reward profiles (flavors), and why eating and understanding feel different despite both being pleasant
files.fullDescription:
While 03-Reward.md answers WHEN reward fires (preconditions), this file answers WHAT KINDS of reward exist and HOW they interact — distilled from a 6,700-line drill with 115+ research sources.

Core distinction: Evaluative Reward (opioid-mediated, requires compilation, the 'deep satisfaction' — food you've cooked yourself, understanding after struggle) vs Direct-State Reward (non-opioid, direct hardware activation — warmth, touch, physiological satiation). Evaluative GATES Direct-State: the compiled layer can amplify or suppress the hardware signal — same food tastes better when earned.

E₀→E₃ Complexity Gradient: E₀ = pure Direct-State hardware reward, E₁ = learned preference (minimal compilation), E₂ = domain expertise reward (requires ~1000h compiled — math insight, craft mastery), E₃ = meta-reward (reward from the architecture itself operating — 'understanding understanding,' recursive satisfaction).

5 Reward Profiles: the 'flavors' that make eating, creative breakthrough, connection, and mastery feel qualitatively different despite using the same opioid system. Each profile = a different combination of E₀-E₃ layers activating simultaneously.

5 Forces Holding Model: conditions for sustained creative arcs (Flow, Challenge, Progress, Meaning, Connection — each Force maps to a specific E-level). Temporal Stack Model: multiple reward types can layer simultaneously, which is why complex activities feel richer than simple ones.

Development trajectory: Direct-State dominant in infancy → Evaluative layer compiles through domain contact. Compilable Architecture is WHY Evaluative reward is architecturally possible.

files.shortDescription: What kinds of dissonance exist and how they differ — the counterpart to reward architecture, covering Evaluative vs Direct-State dissonance, Mismatch Splitting, and clinical implications
files.fullDescription:
Direct counterpart to Reward-Signal-Architecture.md — same architecture, opposite direction. Answers why 'physical pain,' 'fear of bankruptcy,' and 'betrayal by a close friend' are fundamentally different despite all being called 'discomfort.'

Core distinction: Evaluative Dissonance (compiled layer — involves OFC/ACC, requires chunks, can be suppressed or amplified by PFC) vs Direct-State Dissonance (hardware-level — nociception, cold, hunger — bypasses compilation). Same insula gradient, different neurochemistry and direction.

Mismatch Splitting: the placebo/nocebo effect PROVES Evaluative gates Direct-State dissonance — same mechanism as reward direction. Phantom limb = Evaluative Dissonance firing for a Direct-State source that no longer exists.

Asymmetric Transition Speed: reward → dissonance FAST (survival-first), dissonance → reward SLOW (requires full recalibration). This asymmetry explains why negative events have more lasting impact than positive ones.

Clinical implications: dissociation (Evaluative layer offline), alexithymia (Evaluative blocked from PFC), anxiety (Evaluative Dissonance without Direct-State source), chronic pain (Direct-State + Evaluative coupling), body-therapy rationale (Evaluative layer can be accessed through different channels).

files.shortDescription: How individual gaps consume, compete, and evolve — 3 satiation profiles, 5-parameter model, and how entities fill different gaps through matching
files.fullDescription:
Per-gap dynamics — the zoom-in companion to Gap-Distribution-Profile (zoom-out). Covers how individual gaps work over time: how they consume reward, compete with other gaps, and pass through lifecycle stages.

3 Satiation Profiles: Cyclic (periodic need — hunger, touch, rest — fills then re-emerges predictably), Tonic (continuous background maintenance — connection, autonomy — never fully satisfied, just managed), Generative (feed generates more capacity — skills, knowledge, creative — unique: more you fill it, more gap opens). Each has different reward architecture and intervention logic.

5-Parameter observation model per gap: Source × Satiation Type × Reward Type (Evaluative/Direct-State) × Chain Length (direct body contact vs long chain mediated) × Dependency (which other gaps this gap requires to be active).

ENGINE/ROAD/VEHICLE architecture: Hardware provides ENGINE (basic need structure), Collective Arc provides ROAD (which channels are culturally available), Compilation history provides VEHICLE (what actually runs on this person's road — same engine, wildly different vehicles).

Also covers: entity-gap matching (5-step Self-Pattern-Modeling-mediated process for how one person fills another's specific gap direction), existence-based gaps (parental drive = reward from child's existence, not interaction — unique type), technology fill (which satiation types technology can vs structurally cannot fill). 33 citations.

files.shortDescription: Why gaps have a specific direction — the same information rewarding for an expert is meaningless to a novice, and why reward requires directional match not just gap-filling
files.fullDescription:
Formalizes the principle that gaps are not undifferentiated holes — they have a specific DIRECTION determined by the surrounding chunk network. Reward fires not just when 'a gap is filled' but when the stimulus matches the gap's SPECIFIC direction.

Foundational principle: 'Chưa biết = không có gap' — you cannot have a gap for something you have no chunks about. This follows inevitably from the chunk-system architecture and explains most phenomena involving expertise, appreciation, and learning readiness.

2-layer model: Layer 1 = signal mechanism (does VTA fire?), Layer 2 = direction content (does it match THIS specific gap?). Explains why Schultz 1997 prediction error is 'correct but incomplete' — prediction error is necessary but direction match is also required.

Covers: gap direction installation mechanism (how Background-Pattern constrains the gap landscape), 2-Stream × gap direction (Hardware-Stream gaps vs Modeling-Stream gaps form differently), gap direction as the underlying mechanism of by-product match (when B's output matches A's gap direction → by-product match works), and Compiled vs Fresh evaluation processing.

Companion: Gap-Body-Need.md (per-gap dynamics), Gap-Distribution-Profile.md (aggregate per-person landscape).

files.shortDescription: Every person has a unique gap landscape — a 4-axis profile of where their active gaps cluster, how they formed, and how to predict behavioral compatibility between people
files.fullDescription:
While Gap-Direction covers how individual gaps work, this file zooms out: what does the WHOLE GAP LANDSCAPE look like for one person? Gap Distribution Profile = the aggregate picture — where gaps cluster, how deep, how stable, and how they formed.

4 axes: ① Domain Center (which domains hold the major gaps — work / relationships / meaning / aesthetic / play), ② Origin Balance (hardware-origin vs collectively-installed vs personally-compiled — ratio shapes which gaps feel 'mine'), ③ Depth Profile (broad-shallow vs narrow-deep vs mixed — a single deep domain vs spread across many), ④ Stability (stable long-term vs currently shifting — gap landscape shifts during major life transitions).
