4-layer formation model: Collective Arc (era-level shaper — installs available gap channels for a generation) → Schemas (value + identity schemas direct which channels get attention) → Family/Micro environment (specific exposure shapes depth) → Hardware + Experience (individual baseline). Each layer shapes which gaps emerge as primary.

Practical tools: gap distribution overlap predicts by-product match probability (how likely two people generate outputs that match each other's gaps — the mechanism behind compatibility). Life choices = gap distribution navigation (career, partner, community selection all reflect this landscape).

v1.1 adds: Satiation Types per-domain, ENGINE/ROAD/VEHICLE architecture, PFC Budget × shift cost, Technology fill dimension, Pattern Shiftability × stability.

files.shortDescription: Every person has a different set of things they can actually do — a 4-axis supply-side profile that, paired with gap distribution, predicts real behavior
files.fullDescription:
Action-Space = the aggregate SUPPLY-SIDE landscape of what options are actually available to a person at any moment. Complements Gap-Distribution-Profile (demand side). Actual behavior = f(Gap-Distribution DEMAND × Action-Space SUPPLY).

4 axes: ① Compiled Capacity (what you've trained to do), ② Resource Access (what external resources you can access — money, networks, tools), ③ Freedom (what you're permitted to do — legal, social, obligation constraints), ④ Awareness (what options you even know exist — the 'invisible' constraint is most dangerous because 'chưa biết = không có gap').

3 constraint types: Blocked (present but prohibited), Dependent (present but requires another person), Invisible (present but person doesn't know). Predicts 4 behavioral quadrants: High Gap × High Action-Space (motivated + capable → action), High Gap × Low Action-Space (frustrated → trapped behavior), Low Gap × High Action-Space (boredom → exploration), Low Gap × Low Action-Space (apathy).

Formation model mirrors Gap-Distribution: Collective infrastructure → Social position → Education → Hardware baseline. 17+ citations including Sen 1999 Capability Approach, Bandura 1977 self-efficacy, Maier & Seligman 2016 learned helplessness.

files.shortDescription: People literally cannot perceive quality in areas they lack experience — why experts see what beginners cannot, and why copiers diverge from originals over time
files.fullDescription:
Core principle: if you have no chunks about quality dimension X, you have no gap for X → your body is SILENT when X is present or absent. Compilation depth DIRECTLY determines quality visibility — not effort, not attention, not intelligence.

2 types of hidden quality: (1) Domain Expert quality — technical dimensions visible only after deep compiled experience (surgical complication rates: 5.2% vs 14.5% depending on surgeon skill — Birkmeyer 2013; expert radiologists detect in 200ms; sommelier brain regions differ — Castriota-Scanderbeg 2005); (2) Leader/Coordinator quality — organizational dimensions visible only after going through all coordination layers (what a coordinator 'sees' that contributors cannot).

Why copiers diverge: Compiled/Fresh Double Blindness — a copier has neither automatic detection (not compiled) nor deliberate evaluation capacity (no gap for quality dimensions). Output looks similar initially but diverges when subtle directional choices accumulate over time.

Dunning-Kruger reframe: 'you don't know what you don't know' is not a psychological bias — it's architecturally inevitable: metacognition requires chunks about the domain to generate the gap for missing knowledge. The architecture produces this outcome necessarily.

AI era: AI approaches expert-level in some domains (language patterns, code syntax) but lacks the architectural quality perception requiring body-level compilation. Dual Check accuracy = f(compilation depth).

24+ citations including Gibson 1955 perceptual differentiation, Polanyi 1966 tacit knowledge, Chase & Simon 1973 chess chunks.

files.shortDescription: Every reward has a Goldilocks zone — why too little, too much, or the wrong type of reward all cause problems, and 6 specific over-reward mechanisms to watch for
files.fullDescription:
Reward calibration answers: how MUCH reward is appropriate for THIS gap, in THIS person, at THIS time? Core thesis: reward has a Goldilocks zone per-gap, per-person, per-context — and that zone shifts continuously. Cannot be prescribed, only observed and adjusted (dynamic equilibrium).

3 Gap Types × Reward Match: gaps respond differently to reward amount. Too little → gap reinforcement (body escalates demand). Match → satisfaction + gap update. Too much → 6 over-reward mechanisms activate.

6 over-reward mechanisms: (1) Threshold adaptation (baseline shifts up — what was 'good' becomes 'minimum'), (2) Overjustification effect (external reward destroys intrinsic gap — Lepper 1973), (3) Bridge dependency (reward-for-gap-fill becomes reward-for-the-bridge-itself, decoupled from the gap), (4) Baseline shift (what counts as 'enough' permanently changes), (5) Competence-reward mismatch (reward for performance exceeding actual competence → schema distortion), (6) Evaluative/Direct-State imbalance (oversupplying one type while starving the other).

Premature compilation: rewarding exploration before the Generative gap has run its full cycle closes it too early, preventing deeper learning. Collective Calibration: institutional reward systems (grades, salaries, metrics) systematically miscalibrate by using the same scale for everyone.

Parallel to Logic-Feeling-Balance: cannot prescribe — observe + adjust.

files.shortDescription: Vocabulary reference for the entire framework — standardizes 3-tier labels across intra-body and inter-body concepts so every file uses the same terminology
files.fullDescription:
The framework uses dozens of technical terms across body-feedback, valence, inter-body interaction, and chunk dynamics — but without standardized labels, different files had inconsistent naming. This file formalizes the vocabulary convention for all framework files.

Covers 3-tier label system (General → Direction → Specific) applied to 7 concept families: ① Foundation terms (body-need, gap, gap direction, drive), ② Reward labels (Evaluative/Direct-State, E₀→E₃, 5 Profiles), ③ Dissonance labels (parallel structure), ④ Entity-Compiled 3 subtypes, ⑤ Compiled vs Fresh processing labels, ⑥ By-product match / Anti-match, ⑦ 2-Stream Architecture (Hardware-Stream / Modeling-Stream) + 3 Independent Cost Sources + 5-Channel Input Vector.

Problems resolved: 'pleasant/discomfort' had been confusing mechanism-level with feeling-level labels; 'Logic/Feeling' had been confusing observer categories with mechanism categories; inter-body interaction had no standardized vocabulary at all.

Read immediately after Body-Feedback.md. Every file in the framework should follow this vocabulary convention.

files.shortDescription: Drill Phase 1 — body-feedback architecture foundation: dual-pull principle, interface loop, 2-tier calibration, and the body-feedback vs feeling distinction
files.fullDescription:
The foundation drill for the entire Body-Feedback series — establishes 5 architectural concepts that every subsequent drill phase assumes as given.

Covers: (1) Dual-Pull principle (Hardware pull toward smooth baseline vs Domain pull toward adaptation — this tension is architectural, not pathological, and is WHY body signals exist), (2) Interface Loop formal model (Domain ↔ Body ↔ Schema ↔ Action 6-step cycle), (3) 2-tier calibration WHY body signals are trustworthy (Darwinian survival tuning + Hebbian domain-contact feedback), (4) Body-feedback vs Feeling distinction (raw body signal generated subcortically vs PFC observation of that signal through the feeling layer — 2 different levels, different timing), (5) PFC vs Unconscious division of labor (what each can and cannot do — willpower limits have physical basis here).

Also previews H10 (Body-Feedback-Precondition hypothesis) — formalized later in Body-Feedback-Precondition.md. Read this file before 02-Dissonance, 03-Reward, or 04-Integration.

files.shortDescription: Drill Phase 2 — decodes dissonance across the full spectrum with 9 mechanism types, covering daily mini-dissonance, threat, trauma loops, and the hedonic trap
files.fullDescription:
Decodes WHY body generates dissonance signals through case-driven analysis. Assumes 01-Foundation architecture.

Covers 9 dissonance mechanisms: (1) why threat creates dissonance (3-case matrix: resoluble / irresolvable / chronic), (2) why novelty also creates temporary dissonance (cortisol accompanies prediction-delta — 'novelty fatigue' is real), (3) 5 daily mini-dissonance cases everyone experiences, (4) the 3 Genuine Discomfort Sources (nociception / mismatch / recalibration — these are distinct, not one thing), (5-6) Trauma loop (schema negative → no resolution path → Compile-Trap), (7) Hedonic trap (high-status lifestyle → habituation → persistent low-grade dissonance), (8-9) Learning dissonance and Dissonance × PFC interaction.

Key reframe: 'dissonance = bad feeling to eliminate' (wrong) vs 'dissonance = architectural signal for recalibration' (correct). Without dissonance, organism cannot adapt. Fixes target at the root cause, not the signal.

Introduces: 14 intensity levels, 6 source type taxonomy.

files.shortDescription: Drill Phase 3 — reward mechanism decoded through 7 cases, 5 preconditions, and the Ô Tô Paradox (why expensive gifts can completely miss the body)
files.fullDescription:
Decodes the reward mechanism through case-driven analysis. Assumes 01-Foundation + 02-Dissonance.

VTA vs Opioid distinction drilled: VTA dopamine = doorbell (salience alert, NOT reward), opioid = the actual gift behind the door (body-need match). User's initial hypothesis ('VTA fires when chunks link + schema being resolved → reward') validated with framework refinement.

5 Body-Feedback-Preconditions drill-validated through 7 reward cases (C1.1-C1.7): preconditions met → reward fires; any precondition missing → no reward regardless of 'objective value.'

2 calibration paradoxes: (1) Ô Tô Paradox (C2.1-C2.5 — father buys expensive car vs rocket vs vintage car for son → same giver, same cost, wildly different reward because gap DIRECTION differs — not price), (2) Van Gogh Paradox (C3 — appreciation reward requires compiled appreciation chunks — same painting generates nothing for untrained eyes, intense reward for compiled viewer).

Also covers: schema update latency (why reward arrives after, not at, the moment of resolution), chunk association tag mechanism, and reward sustainability conditions. Formalized into Body-Feedback-Precondition.md.

files.shortDescription: Drill Phase 4 — unifies the entire drill series into a running architecture, with 3 end-to-end case walkthroughs (Einstein, hedonic trap, trauma loop)
files.fullDescription:
Integration drill — synthesizes P1-P3 into a coherent single architecture. No new mechanisms, but connects everything into a model that runs as a whole.

3 integration tasks: (1) Unified Interface Loop formal model (all elements — Dual-Pull, Chunk activation, Body-Need, Feeling layer, PFC, Imagine-Final — shown running simultaneously), (2) Role of key players in the cycle (Imagine-Final = pre-body-evaluation simulation that runs BEFORE actual experience; Anchor-Schema = predictive context that shapes signal interpretation; Personal-Melody = emergent trajectory from all chunk interactions), (3) 3 end-to-end case walkthroughs: Einstein-cây-hoa case (spreading activation → Imagine-Final background → mini dissonance emergence), Hedonic trap (why 'having everything' generates persistent low-grade dissonance), Trauma loop (why some dissonance states become self-reinforcing).

Also contains Phase 5 sections: H10 formalization (5 preconditions formalized into Body-Feedback-Precondition.md) and framework update incorporating drill insights. Read after 01-02-03.

files.shortDescription: First-principles drill — why evolution had to design body-feedback exactly this way, derived from sensor design constraints rather than neuroscience observations
files.fullDescription:
A top-down first-principles analysis: instead of 'observe brain → describe architecture,' this drill asks: 'if you were DESIGNING a sensing system from scratch, what would you NEED?' — then deduces what architecture must inevitably emerge.

8 insights from evolutionary constraints: (1) Evolution cannot make bidirectional sensors — no fixed zero-point is achievable via random mutation + selection, (2) Multiple one-way strategy = the only viable solution (TRP channels, CT afferents, nociceptors are all one-directional), (3) Brain integration levels follow from cascade necessity (receptor → spinal → insula → cortex), (4) Baseline is emergent not designed (adaptive zero explains why 'normal' varies per person — and why trauma shifts it permanently), (5) WHY Evaluative layer MUST exist (long-horizon value tracking requires compilation — hardware alone can't do it), (6) Universal approach/avoid = simplest decision architecture that works across all scales (bacteria → humans), (7) Reward/Dissonance symmetry = functional requirement from information theory (asymmetric system loses half its signal), (8) Asymmetric speed in Evaluative layer (reward→dissonance fast = survival-first).

Key insight: every property of body-feedback architecture that seems arbitrary or surprising follows NECESSARILY from these constraints.

files.shortDescription: What 'feeling' actually is — the 7-layer fidelity model, 8-step body-to-PFC flow, and why feeling is the body's primary communication channel (not just emotion)
files.fullDescription:
The central reference file for the feeling system. Defines 'feeling' as the PFC's observation of ongoing body-chunk interaction — not just emotion, but the full feedback channel the body uses to communicate with conscious awareness.

Covers the complete architecture: 7-layer fidelity structure (from raw body signal to articulated verbal label), 8-step operational flow (body fire → integrate → threshold → PFC observe → match chunks → label → body vote → action), and the temporal invariant: body always processes before PFC, this order never reverses.

Key distinctions: feeling vs emotion vs sensation vs intuition vs mood. Explains why ~95% of feeling operates in compiled (automatic) mode and only ~5% reaches active PFC deliberation.

14 sections including cross-references to all sub-files. Gateway file for the entire Feeling folder.

files.shortDescription: How feeling works end-to-end — the 8-step body-to-PFC operational flow, feeling gradient (clear to vague same mechanism), and how quality varies with chunk depth
files.fullDescription:
The deep mechanistic file for how feeling works, step by step. 8-step operational flow: body activates → interoceptive signals integrate → activation crosses threshold → PFC observes signal → PFC matches signal against stored chunks → labeling occurs (or fails) → body votes on label accuracy → action impulse forms.

Gradient insight: clear feelings and vague feelings run on the same mechanism — the difference is chunk library depth and compile context, not a different system. Reframes 'I don't know what I'm feeling' as a chunk-gap phenomenon, not emotional immaturity.

Key sections: threshold dynamics, bidirectional PFC-body interaction, why the body 'votes' on PFC labels, and how feeling recognition quality is trainable. Companion to Feeling.md (architecture) and Feeling-Chunk-Bridge-Draft.md (integration).

files.shortDescription: Where feelings come from — 10+ body signal channels mapped to the feelings they produce, 50+ examples, and 'falling in love' decomposed into 9 simultaneous channels
files.fullDescription:
A comprehensive map of all input channels that generate feelings. 10+ source types: L0 Threat detection, L1 Physiological/interoceptive signals, L2 Experience gap (novelty), Mastery/Meaning/Status markers, Agent-generated input, Mirror-resonance signals, Schema-triggered patterns, Imagine-Final simulation previews, Valence compiled from entity exposure, and Cognitive/prediction signals.

50+ examples spanning all channels, from basic hunger to complex moral discomfort. Case study: 'falling in love' decomposed into 9+ channels firing simultaneously — explains why romantic feeling overwhelms rational analysis and defies simple explanation.

Serves as the source catalogue for the feeling system. Pairs with Feeling-Mechanism-Deep-Draft.md (how signals become feelings) and Feeling-Chunk-Bridge-Draft.md (how they connect back to chunks).

files.shortDescription: Why PFC often misreads body signals — 6 predictable error modes (rationalization, misattribution, displacement, denial, cultural override) and feeling literacy as a trainable skill
files.fullDescription:
Covers how PFC reads feeling signals and the systematic ways it fails. The temporal order is invariant: body processes first, PFC observes after — but PFC frequently misinterprets what it observes.

6 error modes: (1) Rationalization (PFC invents post-hoc story), (2) Misattribution (assigns feeling to wrong cause), (3) Displacement (redirects to safer object), (4) Over-complex labeling (adds unearned specificity), (5) Under-labeling/Denial (blocks signal from reaching verbal), (6) Cultural script override (learned emotional display rules suppress genuine signal).

Pain as special case: physical pain and 'emotional pain' share neural substrates but have distinct body-vote dynamics. Feeling literacy — accurately reading one's own body signals — is a trainable skill, not a fixed personality trait. Companion to Feeling-Literacy-Training-Draft.md.

files.shortDescription: How the chunk system and feeling system integrate — bidirectional mapping where chunk operations produce feelings and feeling states feed back into chunk processing
files.fullDescription:
The integration file making the framework coherent. Establishes that the chunk system and feeling system are the same process observed from two angles ('same elephant, 2 observers').

Production direction: each category of chunk operation generates a corresponding feeling layer. Compilation produces mastery feelings. Chunk-miss produces gap-feelings (incompleteness, unease). Schema activation produces recognition feelings.

Feedback direction: feeling states influence which chunks get activated, how strongly, and which get suppressed. Strong body signal amplifies chunk search; avoidance feeling narrows it. Feeling is not a passive output of chunk processing but an active modulator.

Key implication: improving chunk quality via learning directly improves feeling quality — same substrate, same mechanism.

files.shortDescription: Compiled expertise as instant recognition — how 'knowing without explaining' works, 3 directions (domain/self/social), and Pre-Body-Knowing sub-types
files.fullDescription:
Body-Knowing is the compiled function of the feeling system — instant recognition that arrives before verbal explanation. Defines Body-Knowing as 'compiled chunks + body signal together exceeding PFC observation threshold without deliberate search.'

Three directions operate on the same mechanism: Outward (domain expertise — expert reads the room before anyone explains), Inward (self-awareness — knowing something is wrong before you can say why), Social (reading others — detecting dishonesty or emotional state before any explicit signal).

Pre-Body-Knowing sub-types: vague-knowing (signal below labeling threshold but influencing behavior), hunch (directional signal without specific target), nagging unease (recurrence-triggered without identified source).

Key figure: ~95% of life's navigation runs on Body-Knowing; active PFC deliberation accounts for ~5%. Explains why 'thinking harder' often produces worse decisions in high-compiled domains.

files.shortDescription: How feeling literacy training actually changes the chunk system — 5-stage framework, Gendlin Focusing as mechanism, and why 'just pay more attention' is not enough
files.fullDescription:
Explains how training to read body signals works at the chunk level — not as a behavioral habit but as structural chunk system change. 5-stage framework: (1) Body Awareness (distinguishing signal from noise), (2) Signal Discrimination (identifying quality and gradient), (3) Pattern Recognition (matching recurring signal patterns), (4) Label Attachment (anchoring signals to words), (5) Meta-Feeling (feeling about the feeling process itself).

Gendlin Focusing (the Somatic-Articulation-Loop) is the operational mechanism — the body 'knows' before words do, and training is about closing that gap progressively.

Failure modes: why people who 'try to listen to their body more' often regress (over-verbalization forces premature labeling). Natural Discovery: 3 conditions under which feeling literacy develops without deliberate training.

files.shortDescription: Research foundation for the feeling concept — 7 researcher streams (Damasio, Gendlin, Craig, Panksepp, Barrett, Gallese, Kahneman), 7 distinctions from related terms, 7 applications
files.fullDescription:
The research foundation behind the framework's feeling concept. Maps 7+ major research streams: Damasio (somatic marker hypothesis), Gendlin (felt sense, Focusing method), Craig (interoception and the insula), Panksepp (affective neuroscience), Gallese (embodied simulation), Kahneman (System 1 intuition), and Barrett (emotional granularity and constructed emotion theory).

7 key distinctions: Feeling vs Emotion, vs Sensation, vs Thought, vs Intuition, vs Gut Feeling, vs Mood — each distinction mapped to mechanism differences, not just vocabulary.

7 practical applications derived from the synthesis. Serves as the evidence anchor for Feeling.md's core claims.

files.shortDescription: Feeling capability development outline — 5-axis model, expanded feeling definition as PFC observation interface (Status: partial outline, Phase 4 content pending)
files.fullDescription:
A synthesis outline for how feeling capability develops. 5-axis model: (1) Body signal clarity, (2) Label vocabulary breadth, (3) Tolerance for ambiguous signals, (4) Meta-feeling capacity, (5) Social-feeling calibration.

'Expanded feeling definition': Feeling = PFC observation interface — not a narrow emotional response but the full channel through which body-processed information reaches conscious awareness. Development = enlarging the interface.

Status: OUTLINE — Phase 4 content fill pending. The structure establishes framework and axes; detailed content for each developmental stage not yet written. Companion to Feel-Example-Draft.md (124 concrete examples).

files.shortDescription: 124 feeling examples across the developmental trajectory — from newborn crude distress to adult existential complexity, each mapped to channels, chunks, and developmental stage
files.fullDescription:
A comprehensive example library for the feeling system. 124 examples spanning the full developmental trajectory (E1-E124): newborn crude distress (pre-chunk, pure body signal) through childhood (first schema-tagged feelings) through adult complexity (compound feelings involving status, meaning, existential threat simultaneously).

Each example is mapped to: source channels active, chunk substrate required, axis position on the 5-axis development model, developmental stage, and estimated prevalence.

Status: Phase 2 complete. Particularly useful for the 'adult drive compounds' section (E100-E124) where multiple systems fire simultaneously — concrete anchors for the abstract feeling architecture when mechanisms in other files feel unclear.

files.shortDescription: Cross-theme synthesis of 6 feeling system deep-dives — 40+ sub-questions resolved, core architectural commitment locked, and 6 key surprises documented
files.fullDescription:
The culminating synthesis for the Drill-Feeling-Knowning series. Integrates conclusions across 6 themes (Architecture, Right/Wrong feeling, Verbal Chain, Ritual, Empathy+Giving, Logic-Feeling check) into a coherent framework position.

Core conclusion: framework is internally consistent across all 6 themes. Central commitment locked: 'Feeling = PFC observation of integrated unconscious processing. Body-first temporal invariant. ~95% compiled, ~5% active PFC deliberation.'

6 key surprises documented — discoveries that emerged from the drill that were not anticipated. Framework self-correction: Body-Knowing as the primary mode, PFC deliberation as the rare exception.

files.shortDescription: Raw re-reading notes from all Feeling files — cross-theme mapping, 4 key definition phrases, and vocabulary anchors for the drill series (entry point to Drill-Feeling-Knowning)
files.fullDescription:
The raw material drill file — detailed notes taken during systematic re-reading of all Feeling folder files. Foundation and vocabulary base for all subsequent drill themes (01 through 06).

Contains the formal 4-phrase definition of Feeling extracted during re-reading. Cross-theme mapping identifying which sections from which files connect across the 6 themes.

Not meant for standalone reading — functions as the entry point and shared vocabulary for the Drill-Feeling-Knowning series. ~1,871 lines.

files.shortDescription: Drill — Does feeling belong naturally to the body-base? The meta-frame answer: feeling = PFC observation (bidirectional, not just bottom-up)
files.fullDescription:
A deep-dive drill exploring the fundamental architecture question: 'Is the framework forcing feeling into a body-base model, or does feeling naturally belong there?' Meta-frame analysis testing whether the body-first commitment is imposed or derived.

Conclusion: feeling belongs to body-base + PFC as observer — the model is bidirectional. PFC does not generate feelings but observes them; however, the act of observation and labeling feeds back into body state.

Key sub-questions covered: threshold dynamics, observation quality, why PFC observation changes (but doesn't create) feeling. Sets the architectural foundation for all subsequent themes in the series.

files.shortDescription: Drill — How 'something feels wrong' works mechanically — Einstein case study, 8 sub-questions, ACC mismatch detection producing unease before verbal explanation
files.fullDescription:
A drill exploring the 'right/wrong feeling' mechanism — sensing something is incorrect before being able to articulate why. Uses the Einstein case (sensing a problem with Newtonian physics before formal proof) and children's moral judgment as test cases.

Mechanism: compiled chunk patterns activate → ACC (anterior cingulate cortex) detects mismatch between activated pattern and current context → PFC perceives mismatch as 'something feels off.' 8 sub-questions worked through, from basic pattern violation to complex ethical sensing.

AI relevance: AI systems can detect statistical anomalies but cannot generate body-based mismatch feeling. Explains why 'trusting your gut' has real predictive validity in high-compiled domains — it is ACC mismatch detection, not random intuition.

files.shortDescription: Drill — How chunks and verbal language interact — chunks exist first, words serve as anchors enabling longer chains, grammar as chain-building infrastructure (MEDIUM confidence)
files.fullDescription:
A drill on the verbal-chunk-chain hypothesis: chunks are the primary storage unit; verbal chunks are special-type chunks that serve as anchors enabling longer chains of non-verbal chunks.

Hypothesis (MEDIUM confidence): verbal brain regions develop after chunk accumulation begins; words don't create meaning — they anchor and extend meaning chains. Grammar as chain-builder: grammatical structures allow chains of 30-40 chunks that would otherwise collapse under working memory limits.

Key question: Does verbal labeling compress chunks (chunk-as-word) or extend them (word-as-anchor)? Evidence: pre-verbal infant behavior, aphasia cases, expert wordless knowing. Framework position: verbal = anchor type, not the fundamental unit. Requires external neuroscience validation.

files.shortDescription: Drill — How rituals work mechanically — 3 candidate definitions tested, ritual confirmed as non-verbal chunk sequence for schema anchoring (HIGH confidence)
files.fullDescription:
A drill building on Theme B (Verbal Chain). Tests 3 candidate definitions of ritual: (1) behavioral habit loop, (2) social coordination signal, (3) non-verbal chunk sequence for schema anchoring.

Conclusion (HIGH confidence): Candidate 3 validated. Ritual = a sequenced chain of chunk activations, performed without verbal commentary, that reliably activates and stabilizes a specific schema. Each step in the ritual fires the next chunk in the sequence — by completion, the target schema is fully anchored.

Derivations: why rituals resist modification (changing one step breaks the chain), why verbal explanation of a ritual weakens it, why rituals have cultural preservation power. Connects to Anchor-Schema.md §3 Source ④.

files.shortDescription: Drill — Empathy reframed as Resonance, social liking as schema overlap, and why giving feels joyful vs obligatory (most complex theme in the series)
files.fullDescription:
The largest and most complex theme drill, covering empathy, social liking, and giving dynamics. Core reframe: empathy is not 'feeling what the other feels' but resonance — Self-Pattern-Modeling running on the other person's observable signals. Matches Bird & Cook 2013 (alexithymia as primary empathy deficit).

Social liking mechanism: occurs when two people's chunk-schema patterns overlap enough that being with them reduces prediction cost — their behavior is more predictable, gaps fire more gently.

Giving dynamics: when giving produces joy, the mechanism is gap-closure (seeing someone's gap + having capacity to fill it = body compulsion). When giving produces discomfort, the mechanism is obligation-compilation (gift creates a debt schema). Difference is compiled history, not personality. Cross-references to Empathy.md and Connection.md.

files.shortDescription: Drill — Integration check verifying Logic-Feeling.md consistency with the 6-theme analysis, and whether logic is a subset of expanded feeling
files.fullDescription:
The final theme drill — a consistency check verifying that Logic-Feeling.md conclusions remain valid after all insights from Themes A through E.

Central question (RQ19): Is logic a subset of an expanded feeling definition, or genuinely separate? Framework position (HIGH confidence): logic operations run on chunks, and the 'correctness signal' from valid logical steps is a form of compiled feeling — on the same substrate, not a different system.

Implication: the hard boundary between 'rational' and 'emotional' dissolves at the mechanism level. Both use the same chunk substrate and produce body-signal outputs. The apparent separation is a PFC labeling artifact.

Conclusion: Logic-Feeling.md is consistent with Themes A-E. No revisions needed. Completes the validation loop for the Feeling architecture.

files.shortDescription: Schema redefined as an observation parameter — schema = named chunk-network pattern (how we describe brain activity from outside, not how it actually operates)
files.fullDescription:
Reframes schema from v1.1's architectural component to v2.0's observation parameter. Core distinction: chunks = actual computing units; schema = named description of chunk-network patterns observed from outside (like 'feature' vs. 'function' in software).

Key clarification: the brain doesn't 'run schemas' — it runs chunks. Schemas are useful for description, prediction, and communication but are NOT separate mechanisms.

Covers: what schema IS in v7.8 architecture, observable properties (depth gradient, activation, trigger conditions), schema × body-feedback interaction, schema × PFC, schema × domain, and Body Baseline State — the persistent background setting derived from high-density chunk networks.

Supersedes Schema-Operations.md and Schema-Example.md (v1.1 files backed up). v2.0 COMPLETE.

files.shortDescription: How the unconscious needs a sync point — Anchor-Schema is what the unconscious requires to coordinate, 4 sources that fill it, and what happens when it's absent
files.fullDescription:
Introduces Anchor-Schema — a framework-specific concept defining what the unconscious requires to synchronize. Analogy: an orchestra always playing; without an anchor-schema → cacophony ('bored'/'lost'/anxious); with one → melody.

Key distinction: Imagine-Final = PFC product (top-down, what PFC proposes). Anchor-Schema = unconscious requirement (bottom-up, what the unconscious will accept as sync point). These are different directions of the same process.

4 sources that can fill an anchor: ① PFC-constructed (deliberate planning), ② Hippocampus-retrieved (remembered pattern), ③ Compiled (automatic from prior experience), ④ External-inject (authority/religion/culture provides the anchor).

Covers anchor strength, trust types, cost gradients, and failure modes (both under-anchored = cacophony and over-anchored = harm loop). Framework-specific concept — no direct research uses this exact term; derived from verified sources.

files.shortDescription: 24 concrete Anchor-Schema examples across the spectrum — micro (seconds) to long-term (years), each annotated with anchor source, trust type, cost, domain validity, and outcome
files.fullDescription:
Example library for Anchor-Schema.md. 24 examples spanning from micro-anchors (picking up a glass of water — milliseconds, implicit trust, fully compiled) to long-term anchors (career, relationship, faith — years, explicit trust, domain partially unfalsifiable).

Each example annotated: Anchor (which schema is the sync point), Trust (positive/negative/mixed), Strength (faint/building/compiled), Cost (≈0 to extreme), Domain (real/semi-real/unfalsifiable), Source (①PFC/②Hippocampus/③Compiled/④External-inject), Outcome (melody smooth / temporary dissonance / collapse).

Focuses on the HEALTHY spectrum (v1.2: extreme failure cases moved to Anchor-Schema-Extreme-Example.md). Covers Micro→Short→Daily→Long-Term anchor scales.

files.shortDescription: Extreme cases when Anchor-Schema causes harm — the OVER-anchored failure mode where body signals 'correct' while domain reality is wrong
files.fullDescription:
Specializes in the OVER-anchored failure mode of Anchor-Schema — when an anchor is too strong and domain validity is absent → self-reinforcing harm loop with no body warning signal.

Core structure: two failure modes are symmetric. UNDER-anchored = cacophony, loss, anxiety (covered in main file). OVER-anchored = body says 'correct' + domain says 'wrong' → body wins → ongoing harm without awareness.

Examples: limerence (OCD-adjacent love fixation — body generates complete melody even as real relationship becomes harmful), ideology that feels deeply right despite evidence against it, obsessive planning patterns. Each annotated using the same format as Anchor-Schema-Example.md.

Note: framework provides analysis perspective, not clinical replacement. Clinical assessment required for extreme cases.

files.shortDescription: Personal melody — each person as a unique piece of music, the Melody Lens metaphor for visualizing one's emergent internal state
files.fullDescription:
Foundation file for the Melody Lens — a metaphor-based communication tool for visualizing complex internal states. Each person at each moment = one piece of music playing: all chunks currently firing, compiled, and interacting simultaneously produce an emergent "whole" that is more than the sum of its parts.

Musical vocabulary mapped to framework concepts: notes (individual chunks), harmony (aligned schema), dissonance (chunk-shift/miss), key (compiled baseline), tempo (cortisol-driven pace), instruments (6 modalities as encoding channels). Two-axis tension (Body-Base vs Domain) defines the shape of the melody at any moment. 4 criteria for a "good melody."

Note: Melody Lens is a communication tool, NOT a 1-to-1 neural description. Mechanisms are defined in Core-Foundation, Body-Feedback, and Chunk-System files; this file helps readers visualize and communicate about those mechanisms using everyday language.

Sibling files: Melody-Arc.md (learning cycle detail), Global-Melody.md (collective interaction), Personal-Melody-Example.md (3 illustrated profiles). Read this file before the siblings.

files.shortDescription: Melody arc — the learning cycle of dissonance → compile → upgrade, with design principles and techniques for making the process less painful
files.fullDescription:
Every time someone builds something new, they go through a predictable arc: comfort → dissonance → re-comfort at a higher level. This file explains WHY the arc has dissonance (chunk-miss/shift during compilation) and HOW to design arcs for minimum discomfort — for oneself, for children, for students, or for a team.

9 design principles for arc construction. 6 optimization techniques (breaking large arcs into smaller ones, using Imagine-Final as compass, anchor via precedent). 7-step observer process for managing one's own arc in real time. 6 failure modes showing how arcs collapse.

Chunk dynamics within the arc: Shift (existing chunk fires in wrong context), Miss (expected chunk fails to fire), Gap (chunk connection not yet formed) — each produces distinct body signals. Cortisol acts as amplifier throughout: same arc, different cortisol baseline = very different felt experience.

Requires Personal-Melody.md §4 (Two-Axis) and §5 (Investment Cost) as foundation.

files.shortDescription: Personal melody examples — 3 common life trajectory profiles annotated with observation parameters to help readers recognize their own pattern
files.fullDescription:
Applies Personal-Melody theory to 3 of the most common life trajectory profiles — designed so any reader can find overlap with their own experience. Each profile is annotated with v7.8 observation parameters (Novelty, Connection, Status, Boredom, Threat) and chunk dynamics (Shift/Miss/Gap) at each life phase, rendered using musical notation symbols.

3 profiles: ① Center-aligned trajectory — stable melody, fits social expectations easily, smooth compilation, lower novelty exposure; ② Edge-builder trajectory — high novelty drive, maps new domain, higher dissonance cost and longer arcs; ③ Post-collapse rebuild — melody disruption, recompilation from fragments, anchor reset sequence.

Note: these are 3 life phases or states that can happen at any point — NOT 3 personality types. Most people cycle through all 3 at different times. Confidence level: framework synthesis + illustration (not empirical data).

Read Personal-Melody.md before this file.

files.shortDescription: Global melody — how individual life patterns interact at every scale from family to humanity, using musical metaphor to describe collective dynamics
files.fullDescription:
Explores how individual life patterns (melodies) interact when multiple people coexist, at each zoom level: group (3-500 people) → culture (national level) → global (all of humanity) → domain (each knowledge field). Uses musical metaphor: harmony (mutual resonance), dissonance (pattern conflict), sync (alignment), center vs edge positioning.

Key sections: Group melody dynamics and how roles naturally emerge; Cultural melody keys (why each culture has a distinct baseline); Global arc wave patterns (how attractors spread through a population); Genius vs instability at the Edge (individuals far from center map new domain at high personal cost); AI era effects on global melody patterns.

Note: metaphor communication tool — helps visualize collective dynamics that mechanism files describe technically. Positions and concepts map to framework mechanisms (By-Product-Gap-Resonance, Self-Pattern-Modeling, Status, Conflict-Dynamics) without duplicating them.

Requires Personal-Melody.md as prerequisite.

files.shortDescription: Simulation-Engine — the brain's single general-purpose simulation substrate (DMN + mPFC + insula + hippocampus) underlying all mental modeling applications
files.fullDescription:
Simulation-Engine establishes that the brain does not have separate modules for self-modeling, agent-modeling, future-planning, memory, and moral reasoning — all run on one shared constructive simulation substrate.

Engine = 3 Components: Interoception (anterior insula reads body state), Constructive Simulation (DMN + hippocampus recombines chunks into scenarios), Self/Other Model (mPFC gradient: ventral = self + close, dorsal = distant). Applications are defined by position on 3 Axes: Target (Self↔Other) × Time (Past↔Future) × Operation (Observe↔Construct).

Named applications: Self-Pattern-Modeling = (Other, Present, Simulate); Self-Observation = (Self, Present, Observe); Imagine-Final = (Self, Future, Construct). Decisive proof of shared substrate: Alexithymia — one component failure degrades ALL applications simultaneously. Training one application improves all others.

files.shortDescription: PFC hardware parameters — 4 receptor systems that explain why the same 24 PFC functions produce different outputs across individuals
files.fullDescription:
PFC-Hardware covers the individual-difference layer of PFC function: 4 receptor/enzyme systems that create permanent hardware variation — COMT (Val/Val vs Met/Met: clear speed, improviser vs specialist axis), DRD4 (chunk detection threshold), NE α2/α1 balance (signal-to-noise ratio), and general PFC capacity (volume, myelination).

Covers: 2 main hardware properties (Capacity = how much PFC can hold/process; Quality = signal clarity per slot), how hardware parameters interact with experience and training, and why a hardware ceiling exists but most people operate well below it.

This is the WHY-people-differ file. PFC-Function.md covers WHAT PFC does (same architecture for everyone). Hardware explains output variation — not a quality hierarchy, just different optimization profiles.

files.shortDescription: PFC functions — 24 distinct operations organized into 5 categories that the prefrontal cortex can perform across different activation states
files.fullDescription:
PFC-Function maps what the prefrontal cortex can do: 24 named functions organized into 5 categories — OBSERVE (monitor body + environment), HOLD (maintain dimensions in working memory), PROCESS (transform held content), ORCHESTRATE (direct other brain regions), and STRATEGIC (long-horizon planning).

Covers: each of the 24 functions with confidence level and research grounding, 3 PFC offline cases (why PFC fully shuts down under extreme stress, how recovery differs from normal operation), and how the 5 categories interlock in typical cognitive sequences.

This is the static reference: WHAT PFC does. Companion files cover WHY outputs differ between people (PFC-Hardware.md), HOW functions combine in different brain states (PFC-Configuration.md), and HOW to develop these functions (PFC-Development.md).

files.shortDescription: PFC operations — Hold and Suppress mechanics, Compiled Quality Dimension (genuine/schema/threat), and 3-cost PFC budget model
files.fullDescription:
PFC-Operations covers how PFC actually operates on content: Hold mechanism (4 modes: slot, quick-search, loose-hold, active-lock), Suppress mechanism (inhibiting competing activations), and the Compiled Quality Dimension — 3 types of compiled content: Genuine (domain-verified), Schema (socially-absorbed), and Threat (fear-compiled).

Covers: PFC Budget model (Hold Cost + Suppress Cost + Switch Cost = total budget), why budget depletion explains decision fatigue and ego depletion, Compilable Architecture (how operations progressively reduce cost as content compiles), and the PFC Budget × Emotional State interaction (why stress consumes budget before tasks begin).

Key insight: the Compiled Quality Dimension explains why high-clarity body-knowing can still be inaccurate — schema-compiled and threat-compiled content feels identical to genuine compiled content from the inside.

files.shortDescription: PFC configurations — 6 dynamic modes that change which of the 24 functions are active, enhanced, or disabled in different brain states
files.fullDescription:
PFC-Configuration describes how PFC function combinations shift across brain states: 6 Configuration Modes from Normal (① default) through Flow (② task-immersive), Reallocation (③ resource-shift), Disconnected (④ subcortical takeover under threat), Hyperactivated (⑤ dissociation/PTSD oscillation), and Disintegrated (⑥ psychedelic/near-dissolution).

Covers: PFC Control Spectrum (Overcontrol ↔ Balanced ↔ Undercontrol), 24 Functions × 6 Sub-Regions mapping (many-to-many, dlPFC as hub), Configuration × Function Survival Matrix (which functions survive in which mode), Participation × Configuration as 2 orthogonal taxonomies, and System Reconsolidation (Config ⑥ × Function ⑭ = structural change window).

Key applications: explains ADHD (DMN interference in Config ①), PTSD (oscillation between Config ④ and ⑤), and why therapeutic window timing matters — Reconsolidation requires Config ⑥ or near-⑥ states.

files.shortDescription: PFC development — how PFC capacity grows across lifespan through chunk accumulation, myelination, and pruning, not hardware maturation
files.fullDescription:
PFC-Development challenges the Piaget-era claim that 'PFC is offline in young children.' Five empirical pillars from modern neuroscience (2007-2025) show PFC hardware is active from prenatal — what develops is compiled chunks, myelination speed, and synaptic pruning specificity.

Covers: Hardware vs Compiled Content reframe (behavioral failure ≠ structural absence), PFC developmental trajectory (Worker → Director → Monitor → Compiled phases), Schema Modification mechanism (new schemas layer on top; old schemas are never deleted), PFC Training (hardware ceiling exists but most people operate well below it), and PFC Architecture Ceiling (3 physical constraints: metabolic cost, signal interference, connection latency).

Also covers cross-species encephalization: 4 converging hypotheses on why human PFC expanded. Key implication for education and parenting: 'child has no PFC yet' is a myth — children have PFC hardware, they lack compiled content.

files.shortDescription: PFC working memory capacity — analysis of why ~4±1 dimensions is the convergence point of 6 independent forces, not an arbitrary limit
files.fullDescription:
PFC-Hold-Dimensions reframes the Cowan (2001) ~4±1 working memory limit: not '4 items' but '4 dimensions' — 4 simultaneous parameter axes that PFC holds, compares, and recombines. The reframe matters: adding cognitive load doesn't fill a slot, it changes the topology of the comparison space.

Covers: 6 independent forces all converging at ~4 (oscillation frequency constraint, metabolic cost curve, interference limit, pattern-space topology, evolutionary optimization, and neural architecture constraint), why 2-3 is insufficient (insufficient cross-axis comparison) and 5+ is excessive (most situations lack a fifth meaningful dimension), and the dynamic range (stress shrinks effective working space to 2, flow can expand to 5).

All content is 🟡 framework synthesis — consistent with established data, but the convergence analysis is novel. Honest Assessment section marks confidence per claim.

files.shortDescription: Attention-Spectrum — attention as a continuous multi-factor spectrum from detail-focused to big-picture, not a binary ADHD/non-ADHD classification
files.fullDescription:
Attention-Spectrum reframes attention as a continuous spectrum determined by 4 interacting hardware factors: COMT clear speed (improviser vs specialist axis), chunk threshold (DRD4 hypothesis: detail sensitivity vs big-picture filter), NE sensitivity (signal-to-noise balance), and cortisol state (acute vs chronic baseline).

Covers: each person's position on the detail↔big-picture spectrum, Hyperfocus (high threshold = noise cancelling, not disorder), classroom mismatch (standardized environment optimizes for one spectrum position), methylphenidate mechanism (NE + dopamine: increases signal-to-noise), and AI era implications (AI as spectrum-adaptive working memory partner).

Important: ADHD is a clinical diagnosis with DSM criteria. This file describes the underlying spectrum that ADHD intersects — it is not a diagnostic tool.

files.shortDescription: Logic-Feeling — 'Logic' and 'Feeling' are observer labels for the same Body-Knowing mechanism; the real axis is Compiled↔Fresh processing
files.fullDescription:
Logic-Feeling reframes the intuition-vs-reason debate: both 'logic' and 'feeling/intuition' are observer labels for the same underlying process — compiled chunks firing through Body-Knowing. The real axis is Compiled (automatic, body-direct) vs Fresh (PFC-drafted, costly).

'Logic' = observer label when Body-Knowing output is shareable (deterministic domain). 'Feeling/Intuition' = observer label when Body-Knowing output is non-shareable (probabilistic domain). PFC is not an independent rational agent — it is the Lawyer that rationalizes body-knowing outputs after the fact.

Covers: Body-Knowing definition (compiled chunks firing → body-direct → knowing without explanation), Compiled/Fresh spectrum, Flow (body-knowing ↔ Fresh cycling), Dual Check principle (body = quality controller, domain = final arbiter), 6 analyzed cases, parallel processing, and Object/Agent interaction with the spectrum.

files.shortDescription: Logic-Feeling Balance — epistemological meta-principle explaining why neither Body-Knowing nor Fresh processing is fully reliable and balance cannot be prescribed
files.fullDescription:
Logic-Feeling-Balance is the meta-principle companion to Logic-Feeling.md: given that Body-Knowing (compiled) and Fresh (PFC-drafted) processing are both imperfect, why is it impossible to create a rule for 'when to trust which'?

Covers: why Body-Knowing fails (schema-compiled content can be systematically wrong; threat-compiled content generates self-reinforcing errors; domain limits — no body can know what it hasn't experienced), why Fresh processing fails (PFC budget depletion; confabulation; overthinking disrupts genuine compiled patterns), and why any attempt to prescribe 'trust logic when X, trust feeling when Y' is epistemologically self-defeating.

Core argument: the only honest answer is domain feedback as the final arbiter — outcomes in the domain reveal which processing mode was more accurate. That calibration process IS the core value of the human agent, and cannot be outsourced.

files.shortDescription: Logic-Feeling Failure Examples — evidence companion with real historical and everyday cases where logic failed, feeling failed, and both failed simultaneously
files.fullDescription:
Logic-Feeling-Failure-Examples is the evidence file for Logic-Feeling-Balance.md: 18+ verified real-world cases (historical paradigm shifts, scientific errors, social phenomena, everyday decisions) demonstrating that both logic-driven and feeling-driven processes fail — not as exceptions but as patterns.

Organized into 4 categories: Logic-Fails (historical paradigm shifts, consensus errors, overcalculation), Feeling-Fails-Extreme (cases of strong conviction leading catastrophically wrong), Feeling-Fails-Subtle (everyday schema-driven misjudgments), and Both-Fail-Together (when logic and feeling agree and both are wrong — the most dangerous class).

Not a prescriptive guide — only presents verifiable evidence so readers can recognize: 'this kind of failure is real and happens to intelligent, well-intentioned people — including me.'

files.shortDescription: Logic-Planning — packaged overview of the logic processing side: anchor/label systems, planning mechanism, and why AI excels at logic while humans retain the feeling-side advantage
files.fullDescription:
Logic-Planning packages the 'logic side' of the framework: what logic processing is (chaining labeled chunks sequentially), how anchor/label systems work (3-tier label hierarchy, 5 communication format systems), and how planning chains labeled chunks into Imagine-Final scenarios.

Covers: Logic as map (accurate, detailed, but only covers what's already been labeled), Planning mechanism (chain → goal → simulate → execute), why plans must match real-world complexity (domain is always more complex than the map), AI-assisted logic (AI can chain labeled chunks faster and more consistently — this is the core AI advantage), and Bridge to Feeling (logic labels build ON TOP of body-knowing, not independent of it).

Declaration: Logic side = AI-assisted territory. Feeling side = human-irreplaceable. This file intentionally closes the logic side as 'well-covered, AI can help' to redirect attention to the feeling-side files.

files.shortDescription: PFC-Label Convention — vocabulary reference standardizing PFC-related terms across 78+ framework files using a 3-tier label system
files.fullDescription:
PFC-Label Convention is the vocabulary reference for all PFC-related concepts — companion to Body-Feedback-Label v2.0 (body signal vocabulary). It formalizes which labels to use, what they mean, and when to use them, standardizing terminology that previously varied across 78+ files.

Covers 13 label domains: Roles (Observer/Lawyer/Director/Worker/Universal-Resource), Processing spectrum (Compiled↔Fresh), Operations (Hold/Suppress), Cost (3-cost + budget), Quality (Genuine/Schema/Threat), Regions (anatomy mapping), Hardware (individual differences), Simulation-Engine labels, Cognitive operations, Failure modes, Observer vs Mechanism distinction, and Deprecated terms.

Not a mechanism explanation file — source files explain HOW. This file only formalizes: WHICH label, WHAT it means, WHEN to use it.

files.shortDescription: Self-Observation — APPLICATION-3 on the Simulation-Engine (Self × Present × Observe): the keystone capacity whose failure cascades into 5+ other systems
files.fullDescription:
Self-Observation is APPLICATION-3 on the Simulation-Engine, defined by coordinates (Target=Self, Time=Present, Operation=Observe). Core formula: Self-Observation = f(Interoception × PFC_Encoding × Attention_Direction=SELF). It is what Feeling becomes when attention turns inward — the same mechanism, different target.

Trigger Principle v2: 3 delta types (Body-State shift, Chunk-Fire unexpected, External disruption) × 2 pathways (Reactive = delta-triggered, Proactive = intentional attention shift). Meditation is the canonical proactive mode shift.

2-Axis Model: Development Capacity (hardware ceiling + compiled depth) × Activation Level (current moment engagement). Gradient Mức 0-6: from Body-React (Mức 0, no PFC encoding) through Compiled-Observe (Mức 6, calibrated observation with domain feedback integration).

Keystone property: Self-Observation failure cascades into 5+ systems — Self-Pattern-Modeling accuracy degrades, Feeling literacy stalls, Entity-Access calibration weakens, Body-Knowing formation slows, Logic-Feeling balance cannot be maintained. This makes it a keystone capacity despite not being a separate module.

Critical reframe: Self-Observation is a TOOL, not a VIRTUE. Mức 2 is sufficient for routine life. Depth beyond Mức 2 has diminishing returns and 4 costs (time, rumination risk, social friction, maintenance). Domain feedback — not depth — is the differentiator between useful and excessive self-observation.

Hardware factors: attention allocation (COMT, DRD4), PFC budget (shared resource), compiled quality (genuine vs schema), compiled suppress (blocks signal). Honest assessment: 12 claims 🟢, 18 claims 🟡, 6 claims 🔴. 16 open questions (GAP-SO-1 through GAP-SO-16). 27 citations (R1-R27). Formalized from Drill-Self-Observation v3.2 (1,351 lines, 35 insights).

files.shortDescription: Imagination — process overview of how PFC uses the Simulation-Engine: fidelity levels, 7 cortisol × mode states, and 5 modality types across hardware profiles
files.fullDescription:
Imagination is the process overview for the Imagination/ folder — covering HOW PFC uses the Simulation-Engine to run simulations, with content unique to this file not found elsewhere.

Covers: simulation fidelity (body preview runs at 20-60% fidelity of real experience — cortisol modulates this more strongly than opioids), 5 modalities × hardware profile (COMT/DRD4 affect which sensory channels dominate during imagination), 7 Cortisol × Imagine modes (from IDLE through FLOW to CRASH states), Disconnect mechanism (under threat imagination becomes narrowed or hyperspecific), Override body spectrum (when PFC can push simulation against body resistance), and 3 main use modes (Visualization / Worry / Daydream).

Relation to other files: Simulation-Engine.md = the ENGINE architecture. Imagine-Final.md = the PRODUCT (what the engine produces). This file = the PROCESS (how the engine is driven, with what fidelity, in which state).

files.shortDescription: Imagine-Final — APPLICATION-2 on Simulation-Engine: constructive future simulation that builds scenarios from chunks rather than replaying hardware drives
files.fullDescription:
Imagine-Final is APPLICATION-2 on the Simulation-Engine: the mechanism for constructing future scenarios using chunk recombination — distinct from hardware-driven body prediction (thirst and hunger are hardware drives; 'I will build a company' is Imagine-Final).

Covers: the boundary between hardware prediction and constructive Imagine-Final, 3-layer model (Body-Need drives → Imagine-Final constructs → Plan executes), 5 lifecycle phases (Seed → Build → Anchor → Reload → Compile), 5 gradient levels of clarity (Wish ~0-10% → Obsession ~95-100%), 3 independent dimensions (Clarity × Quality × Trust — all orthogonal), Entity-Access × Imagine-Final (close entities appear in scenarios; their access gradient shapes scenario plausibility), and Engine use quality (Reflection vs Rumination distinction).

Key reframe: Clarity ≠ Quality ≠ Trust. A highly clear Imagine-Final can be wrong or mistrusted; a vague one can be accurate and highly trusted. These three dimensions move independently.

files.shortDescription: Imagine-Final Evaluation — quality assessment framework for future scenarios using 2 axes (Domain Fit × Hardware Fit) plus Trust as a 3rd independent dimension
files.fullDescription:
Imagine-Final-Evaluation provides the quality assessment layer for Imagine-Final scenarios: given that a scenario is clear, is the direction it points toward actually good? Assessment uses 2 primary axes: Domain Fit (how well the scenario aligns with domain reality and attractor points) × Hardware Fit (how well it aligns with the person's actual hardware profile and constraints).

Covers: 4 quality quadrants from the 2-axis intersection (Sweet Spot / Mismatch / Overreach / Delusion), Trust as a 3rd independent dimension creating 8 clusters in 3D space, Delusion sub-case (Hardware-First Harm pattern = high clarity + high trust + poor domain fit), re-evaluation timing (Lifecycle Phase 4 RELOAD = natural quality check window), and long-term outcomes (COMPILE / ACHIEVE & FORGET / ABANDON based on quality + trust trajectory).

Paired with Imagine-Final.md (mechanism of clarity and lifecycle) and Anchor-Schema.md (Trust dimension management).

files.shortDescription: Somatic-Articulation-Loop — the recursive mechanism by which body-knowledge (implicit, compiled) converts into explicit verbal knowledge through articulation
files.fullDescription:
Somatic-Articulation-Loop describes the transition mechanism: compiled body-knowledge (felt-sense, pre-verbal knowing) → explicit communicable knowledge. The process is NOT retrieval — articulation catalyzes new structural access, not just readout of stored content.

Covers: 3 states of knowledge (Implicit = body knows but no words; Transitional = partial words, feels 'close but not right'; Explicit = words click, recognition fires), the loop mechanism (articulation attempt → body response: warmer/colder → refine words → loop → breakthrough), 3 types of external catalyst (human dialogue, writing/journaling, AI as 24/7 catalyst), Gendlin Focusing connection (50+ years of established phenomenological research), AI era variation (AI as articulation partner changes the access economics), and barriers to recognition.

Foundational insight: 'vague feeling' is not vagueness — it is body-knowledge without vocabulary yet. Articulation is the process of finding words that match the pre-existing structural pattern.

files.shortDescription: Integration file — how Novelty, Threat, Boredom, and other body signals combine at each moment to produce the energy and direction of any behavior
files.fullDescription:
Drive is not a separate module — it's the name for the observable energy + direction that emerges when multiple body-feedback signals (Novelty pull, Threat push, Status calibration, Gap-Body-Need pressure) combine at any given moment.

This is an integration file — doesn't introduce new mechanisms, but shows how individual observation parameters interact. Drive direction = f(signal strength × chunk activation × cortisol direction tag × PFC mode × current satiation state).

Covers: 6 drive modes (Auto-Reward / Compiled-Motivated / PFC-Active / Mixed / Depleted / Spinning), signal strength model (which signal 'wins' when multiple drives compete), PFC participation spectrum (from ~5% automatic to ~85% deliberate), compiled vs fresh drive (compiled = effortless automatic, fresh = costly PFC-directed), and drive conflict (when body wants X while PFC wants Y).

Key insight: 'motivation' = observable pattern when compiled drive and PFC direction align. When they don't align, it's not 'laziness' — it's two systems outputting different signals.

files.shortDescription: The 'new = interesting' pattern explained — how VTA detects prediction gaps, 2 forms of novelty (sensory vs imagination-driven), and why some people chase breadth vs depth
files.fullDescription:
Novelty is not a module — it's the name for the observable pattern when VTA detects a positive prediction delta (actual input exceeds what existing chunks predicted).

Covers: the underlying mechanism (VTA seismograph, not reward-dispenser), 2 forms (Sensory-Driven = actual new stimuli; Imagination-Driven = new simulations generating their own prediction gap), the 'novelty brake' (why it self-limits when cortisol stays elevated), novelty loop dynamics (how novelty seeking can become self-reinforcing), and DRD4 gene variant (why some people naturally seek breadth across many domains while others seek depth in one).

Key insight: 'novel' is always defined relative to existing chunks — what's novel for one person is boring for another. Also covers: novelty × cortisol direction gate, chunk habituation (how novelty fades as chunks compile), and practical implications for learning, motivation, and reward calibration.

files.shortDescription: Why modern anxiety feels different from ancient fear — threat as anticipation-dominant dissonance, 5 severity levels × 3 axes, and 3 origin types (domain/peer/imposed)
files.fullDescription:
Threat = the name for body-feedback dissonance directed toward predicted harm. Key distinction: physical threat (bounded, has endorphin buffer, ends in minutes) vs. modern anticipation-threat (unbounded, no endorphin buffer, can last months).

Covers: mechanism (dissonance from predicted gap toward loss), 5 severity levels × 3 axes (temporal: acute vs chronic; origin: domain/peer/imposed; mechanism: Evaluative vs Direct-State Dissonance), why modern life is anticipation-dominant, Threat × Imagine-Final (how simulation amplifies threat without new information), and compiled vs fresh threat (phobias = compiled automatic, novel danger = PFC-evaluated, costly).

3 origin types are central for education: Domain-origin (real danger), Peer-origin (social evaluation), Imposed-origin (authority-generated, compiles 'authority = danger' schema). Imposed threat in education is especially damaging because it can compile permanently.

Also covers: PFC = Lawyer in threat (can suppress, amplify, or fabricate), cortisol interaction, and when threat is genuinely useful vs. when it impedes learning.

files.shortDescription: What 'bored' actually means — 6 sources, 3 types, and why 'bored of each other' has a fundamentally different mechanism from being bored alone
files.fullDescription:
Boredom is not one state — it's a name for several distinct conditions that all share: body-feedback dissonance + low Imagine-Final clarity (no clear direction to move toward). Like 'pain,' the word is accurate but doesn't say where or why.

Covers: 6 sources of boredom (under-stimulation, task-skill mismatch, trapped, repetition habituation, meaning gap, and source ⑥ — by-product match stopping), 3 boredom types (restless / trapped / empty), 6 observation axes (Awareness, Social, Chronic/Acute, Abundance, Agency, Direction), and a unified formula for all types.

Source ⑥ is key for relationships: 'bored of each other' is NOT the same as boredom alone — it happens when by-product match stops (Resonance Decline: 2 Forces + 1 Fuel + Gap Drift). Two people can each be interesting individually but still generate this boredom when gap-filling outputs no longer overlap.

Also covers: boredom as an abundance phenomenon (historically rare, now common), PFC idle-state dynamics, and interventions categorized by boredom source.

files.shortDescription: Human connection has 3 generative sources and 8 pathways — why loneliness is body-level dissonance, and what makes bonds last vs. decay through Resonance Decline
files.fullDescription:
Connection = the name for the observable pattern when the body's social processing systems engage with another person across an Entity-Access gradient. NOT just emotional bonding — includes functional relationships, coordination structures, and parasocial connections.

3 Generative Primitives: ❶ Hardware (oxytocin, μ-opioid, social pain circuits), ❷ Self-Pattern-Modeling (using your own chunks to simulate the other person), ❸ Valence (accumulated body assessment of this specific entity). All 3 must participate for deep connection.

Covers: 8 reward pathways from the 3 primitives, 2-Stream Architecture (Hardware-Stream vs Modeling-Stream), Resonance Decline mechanism (2 Forces: Compiled-Suppress + Reward-Habituated, 1 Fuel: novelty threshold, + Gap Drift), 4-Layer Sustainability (conditions → modality → amplification → trajectory), and 'loneliness' as body-level social dissonance (not just 'being alone').

Also covers: gap clone impossibility (why sharing experience doesn't transfer the gap itself), per-entity dynamics, PFC budget constraints, phantom resonance (why feelings persist after separation), and technology's impact.

files.shortDescription: Empathy is a compiled skill, not a hardware module — how Self-Pattern-Modeling plus positive valence creates it, why it depletes, and why burnout is not 'too much empathy'
files.fullDescription:
Empathy = the observable pattern when Self-Pattern-Modeling (simulating someone else using your own chunks as a template) fires with positive valence and strong entity-access. Not a dedicated hardware module — same mechanism with negative valence = schadenfreude.

Covers: 3 generative primitives (❶ Hardware social circuits × ❷ Self-Pattern-Modeling × ❸ Valence), PFC budget constraint (Fresh empathy costs working memory slots and competes with every other activity), Compiled Quality types (genuine/schema/threat — determines whether empathy sustains or becomes routine), Compilation Chain (Fresh → Compiled → Baseline → Dynamics stages).

Key reframe: empathy 'burnout' is NOT caused by 'too much empathy.' It's caused by Compiled-Suppress + PFC depletion, typically from professionally required empathy that can't be genuine. The fix is different for each type.

Also covers: per-entity empathy profiles (parent→child, best friend, romantic, professional — each with different hardware-subsidy and satiation dynamics), Empathic Accuracy vs Motivated Inaccuracy, and 4-Layer Sustainability for long-term empathic practice.

files.shortDescription: Status is a resource access map, not a rank — why the body gives genuine reward for status gains, the animal→human spectrum, and cooperation as the dominant mode
files.fullDescription:
Status is not about ego — it's the observable pattern when the body's resource-access calibration system evaluates relative position among social agents. Why? Because higher resource access = better body-base feeding = genuine survival advantage. The reward is real.

Key reframe: Status = Resource Access Map (multi-dimensional, per-person, per-context), not a single social rank. Body scans access across dimensions (knowledge, respect, positional power, economic, physical, skill) rather than computing one number.

Covers: evolutionary WHY (resource access = body-base reward), body scan mechanism (unconscious calibration, serotonin = certainty bias), animal→human spectrum (solitary animal: pure rank → primate: hierarchy with coalition → human: multi-dimensional), 3 interaction modes (Take / Exchange / Comply), cooperation as the evolutionarily dominant mode (not competition), Disruption→Recalibrate cycle, and conflict at scale.

Also covers: serotonin + chunk dynamics, cortisol interaction, and the status-meaning interaction ('achieved success but feels empty' = status gain without meaning anchor).

files.shortDescription: Loss aversion and ownership — why losing something hurts more than gaining it, and how the 'protect' pattern spans from objects to people to abstract identities
files.fullDescription:
Protect = the observable pattern when compiled ownership chunks + asymmetric prediction-delta generate protective behavior. Formula: f(perceived replaceability × attachment depth).

Why does loss hurt more than gain? The body evaluates losses asymmetrically (Kahneman endowment effect) — this is not irrational, it reflects that losses are more survivally salient.

Covers: mechanism (ownership chunks compile via repetition + possession patterns), spectrum from objects (low attachment) → people (high) → abstract ideas → identity (where loss = existential threat), vasopressin (ownership/territorial hormone) + cortisol interaction, Gap→Miss dynamics (when owned item disappears from prediction), healthy vs toxic protect (when does protect become possessiveness?), and protect × giving balance (natural counterweight: Agent-Mechanism giving reward override).

Key insight: the same mechanism explains protecting a pen, a person, a belief, and a self-concept. Interventions must match the compilation depth — verbal reframing won't change deep ownership chunks.

files.shortDescription: Why people differ so much in their need for control — how autonomy tendency develops through 5 phases from infancy to adulthood, and what builds vs. damages it
files.fullDescription:
Autonomy tendency = the observable pattern of how strongly a person prefers self-action vs. external-control. Everyone has the same hardware reward for self-action (Autonomy-Hardware.md), but the degree varies enormously by experience.

Mechanism: motor chunks → accumulated evidence of controllability → 'self-as-agent' meta-chunk (compiled belief that 'I can affect outcomes') → domain-specific autonomy strength. Same hardware, radically different compiled software.

Covers: 5 developmental phases (prenatal movement → early reaching/crawling/'NO!' arc → childhood domain mastery → adolescent identity → adult meta-autonomy), domain specificity (high autonomy in work, low in relationships, or vice versa), natural counterweights to extreme autonomy, healthy vs. toxic autonomy patterns, and how imposed threat in childhood compiles 'my opinions are worthless' schema.

Key insight: 'helicopter parenting' → learned helplessness compiles specific chunk patterns that operate automatically for decades. Interventions must re-compile, not just reassure verbally.

files.shortDescription: Why choosing for yourself feels better — efference copy mechanism, vmPFC/DRN controllability learning, and why autonomy preference is universal across all species
files.fullDescription:
Explains the hardware mechanism behind autonomy preference — why self-directed action generates more body reward than externally-controlled action, even when the action itself is identical.

Mechanism: efference copy (the brain sends a copy of its motor command to predict sensory feedback). Self-generated → prediction accurate → micro-opioid reward. Externally controlled → prediction inaccurate (body didn't send the command) → no accuracy-reward even if outcome is same.

Covers: efference copy architecture (universal — all vertebrates have it), vmPFC + DRN controllability learning (Maier & Seligman 2016 — learned helplessness at the neural level), cortisol direction tag (self-action cortisol → approach; external-control cortisol → avoidance), 2-layer dissonance when autonomy is violated, and cross-species evidence.

Key insight: autonomy reward is architectural, not cultural. Companion: Autonomy.md explains why people differ in autonomy tendency despite the same hardware.

files.shortDescription: Meaning as a life-level anchor — 5 types of anchor that give life direction, why coherence is the output not the definition, and what 'meaninglessness' actually is
files.fullDescription:
Meaning = the observable pattern when a person has a stable life-level Anchor-Schema the body accepts as a sync point for the entire chunk system. 'Meaninglessness' = absence of a functioning life-level anchor — chunks become fragmentary because nothing organizes around them.

Key reframe from v1.0: coherence (the feeling of meaning) is the OUTPUT of having a good anchor, not the definition. The mechanism (anchor-schema) matters more than the output description.

5 types of life-level anchor: GOAL (specific long-term target), STATE (ongoing condition to sustain), IDENTITY (self-concept to maintain), FAITH (framework that explains everything), ROLE (function relative to others). Each has different formation, stability, and disruption patterns.

Covers: trigger mechanism (anchor absent / exhausted / disrupted → 'meaning crisis'), Frankl reframe (found meaning in concentration camp = schema reframe, body-inputs unchanged), differential with Boredom (Type 3 Empty = gateway to meaning question), and AI era implications (AI can surface anchor conflicts PFC has rationalized away).

files.shortDescription: Obligation is a social exchange tracking mechanism — compiled prediction of access costs, 6 types from explicit contracts to implicit debt, and why money is obligation technology
files.fullDescription:
Obligation = the observable pattern when compiled predictions about 'the cost to maintain access to this agent' fire. Not a moral feeling — it's the body's exchange tracking system that evolved to calibrate social relationships.

Key reframe: Obligation ≠ Status (what you HAVE), ≠ Threat (what you FEAR losing), ≠ Autonomy (what you CAN do). Obligation = what you SPEND to maintain access.

Covers: 4-stage formation mechanism (receive → experience gap fill → body track cost → compile prediction), 5-factor formula + Status Gap multiplier (higher-status giver = higher perceived obligation), 6 obligation types from explicit-bounded (clear contract) to implicit-unbounded (cultural moral debt), access cost and mode shift (when you ask a favor from someone of higher status), and 3 independent mechanisms that interact.

Money = Obligation Technology: makes implicit obligations explicit, transferable, and quantifiable. Community O(1): collective ritual can maintain shared obligation at near-zero cost. Also covers: parenting → obligation calibration, cross-parameter interactions, and healthy vs. toxic obligation dynamics.

files.shortDescription: Gratitude requires 9 simultaneous conditions — why it's rare, why 'thank you' often isn't real gratitude, and the 3 anti-habituation mechanisms that keep it alive
files.fullDescription:
Gratitude = the observable pattern when 9 components from multiple sub-systems fire SIMULTANEOUSLY — including genuine body-base reward, PFC comparison delta, source attribution to an agent, agent-exceeding-expectation assessment, low cortisol, and active Self-Pattern-Modeling.

Key insights: (1) All 9 must fire at once — removing any one breaks the pattern. (2) 'Social thank-you' is a PFC narrative; body may not fire at all. (3) Gratitude only fires toward AGENT-entities (not toward chance or nature), which is why religion uses personified agents. (4) Without 3 anti-habituation mechanisms (Variation + Comparison + Ritual), gratitude fades even toward the most important people.

Covers: evolutionary WHY (anti-habituation device for connection maintenance), Gift-Obligation Spectrum (pure gratitude ↔ pure debt), 'thank you' as body-base tool with 5 active chains, community-level gratitude at O(1) cost (collective ritual preserving resource-allocation memory), and cases including grateful-to-manipulator (gratitude can be domain-wrong).

files.shortDescription: Bridge from Berridge's wanting/liking model — 'wanting' has 6 underlying mechanisms in this framework, and 'liking' is conditional on 5 preconditions
files.fullDescription:
A bridge file for readers who know Berridge-Robinson (1998) wanting/liking distinction — maps their framework to this one, then shows where the deeper analysis leads.

Framework endorses Berridge: dopamine ≠ reward, wanting and liking are dissociable. But goes further: 'wanting' is not one signal — it's an observable output of 6 distinct underlying mechanisms (VTA salience, prediction gap, Imagine-Final simulation, entity-access gradient, cortisol direction gate, compiled prediction patterns). 'Liking' corresponds to Evaluative Reward only (opioid-mediated, contingent on 5 preconditions) — Direct-State Reward is a category Berridge doesn't address.

Covers: Berridge endorse + extend analysis, 5 Body-Feedback-Preconditions for liking, wanting decomposed into 6 mechanisms, 2 wanting paths (Path A opioid vs Path B relief from cortisol drop).

NOTE: This is a bridge file, not a framework tool. Framework analysis uses Imagine-Final + Body-Base + VTA 7-step + Valence chain, not wanting/liking terminology.

files.shortDescription: How AI can help identify personal mental patterns — a 3-layer model and 9 individual detection capabilities, including critical AI trust guardrails
files.fullDescription:
A gateway file for practical application of the framework with AI support — covers 9 individual-level capabilities for AI-assisted schema detection, plus fundamental limits on what AI can reliably detect.

9 AI capabilities: ①-④ schema pattern recognition (patterns in language), ⑤ compile type detection (Experience/Expertise/Trust), ⑥ valence chain detection, ⑦ conflict detection (PFC vs body), ⑧ collective chain break detection (individual vs. collective source), ⑨ 3-level confidence tagging (Observed / Inferred / Hypothesis).

3-layer deployment model: AI detect → Expert feel-check → Client self-verify. Simplified 2-layer: AI detect → Client self-verify. Self-Drill Mode: user + AI alone, without expert.

Critical §8 AI Trust Guardrails: AI itself can install new patterns (Trust Compile) when the user trusts it — AI output isn't neutral. The paradox: the tool that helps detect schemas can itself be a schema installer. Requires systematic guardrails.

Companion: AI-Collective-Detection.md covers capabilities ⑩-⑭ (collective-level detection).
